Write your multi-paragraph response in the space provided.

title: The Keeper of the Northern Lights

passages.title: The Keeper of the Northern Lights
passages.text:
(1) Long before people measured time with clocks or mapped the stars with telescopes, the people of the Far North gathered around fires and told stories about the lights that danced across the winter sky. They believed the shimmering ribbons of green, blue, and violet were not merely lights at all but part of a great mystery woven into the world by powerful beings.

(2) According to one ancient legend, the sky was once dark every night. The moon and stars provided only a faint glow, and travelers often became lost while crossing snowy forests and frozen rivers. Villages remained isolated during the long winter months because few people dared journey far from home after sunset.

(3) High above the clouds lived Lumara, the Guardian of the Sky. She watched over the world from a palace carved from ice crystals that sparkled like diamonds. Lumara carried a staff made from a fragment of the first star ever created. With it, she could shape winds, guide clouds, and paint colors across the heavens.

(4) Although Lumara cared deeply for the people below, she followed an ancient rule established by the Sky Council. The guardians were forbidden from interfering directly in human affairs. They could observe, but they could not change the course of events.

(5) One winter, however, an unusually fierce season descended upon the land. Snowstorms blocked mountain passes, rivers froze solid, and darkness lingered longer than ever before. Villages struggled to trade with one another, and travelers frequently lost their way.

(6) Among the people was a young hunter named Tarek. One evening, while returning home through a snowstorm, he became separated from his companions. Thick clouds covered the stars, and the darkness was nearly complete. For hours he wandered through the wilderness, unsure of which direction would lead him home.

(7) From her palace in the sky, Lumara watched Tarek struggle against the storm. She wanted to help but knew the rules forbade intervention. As she observed, she realized that Tarek was not the only one in danger. Across the region, countless travelers faced the same challenge.

(8) Unable to ignore their suffering, Lumara climbed to the highest tower of her palace. There she raised her staff and swept it across the heavens. Brilliant streams of color burst into the night sky. Green waves shimmered above the forests, blue ribbons stretched across frozen lakes, and violet arcs curved over distant mountains.

(9) The lights illuminated the landscape below. Travelers suddenly found familiar landmarks. Hunters located safe paths home. Villages saw one another's signals from miles away. Guided by the glowing sky, Tarek safely returned to his family.

(10) News of the mysterious lights spread quickly. People marveled at the beauty above them and celebrated their safe journeys. For the first time in months, communities felt connected despite the harsh winter.

(11) The Sky Council soon learned what Lumara had done. The guardians gathered in the Hall of Stars and demanded an explanation.

(12) "You broke the ancient rule," one guardian declared. "The sky was not meant to guide human lives."

(13) Lumara stood quietly before them. "Perhaps," she replied, "but what purpose does power serve if it cannot be used to help others?"

(14) The council considered her words carefully. They could not deny that her actions had protected countless people. Nor could they ignore the hope the lights had brought to the world below.

(15) After many days of discussion, the council reached a decision. Lumara would not be punished. Instead, the rule would be changed. The guardians could use their gifts to help humanity, provided they acted with wisdom and compassion.

(16) From that day forward, Lumara continued painting the night sky during the darkest winter months. The lights reminded people that they were not alone, even during difficult times. Travelers learned to follow the glowing colors when storms threatened to hide the stars.

(17) Generations passed, and the story of Lumara became part of local tradition. Parents shared the legend with their children, who eagerly searched the sky whenever colorful lights appeared overhead.

(18) Some believed the lights were Lumara's gift of guidance. Others said they were a reminder that compassion can be more powerful than rules. Regardless of how the story was told, the lesson remained the same.

(19) The people understood that true leadership often requires courage, empathy, and a willingness to help others. Just as Lumara had chosen kindness over fear, individuals could choose to use their own talents to improve the lives of those around them.

(20) And so, whenever the northern lights dance across the sky, some still remember the ancient guardian who transformed darkness into hope and showed that even the brightest powers are most meaningful when shared with others.

questions.text:
Which theme is best developed throughout the legend of Lumara?
A. People should avoid changing traditions under any circumstances.
B. True leadership involves using one's abilities to help others, even when doing so requires courage.
C. Natural events are impossible for humans to understand.
D. Rules are always more important than compassion.
Answer: B

How does the harsh winter setting influence the plot of the story?
A. It causes Lumara to lose her magical powers.
B. It creates dangers for travelers, leading Lumara to decide to help the people below.
C. It forces the Sky Council to leave the Hall of Stars.
D. It prevents Tarek from returning to his village permanently.
Answer: B

Which TWO details from the story best support the idea that Lumara is motivated by compassion?
A. She lives in a palace carved from ice crystals.
B. She watches Tarek and realizes that many travelers are in danger.
C. She raises her staff and fills the sky with colorful lights to guide people home.
D. She owns a staff made from a fragment of the first star.
E. She lives high above the clouds.
Answers: B, C

Which statement best summarizes the story?
A. A young hunter becomes lost in a storm and eventually finds his way home after many days of wandering.
B. Lumara, a guardian bound by ancient rules, chooses to help travelers during a dangerous winter by creating lights in the sky. Her actions save people, lead the Sky Council to change its rules, and leave a lasting lesson about compassion and leadership.
C. The Sky Council debates whether guardians should continue living in the sky, and Lumara decides to leave her palace.
D. Villagers become fascinated by colorful lights in the sky and create new traditions to celebrate them.
Answer: B

writingPrompt.text: In "The Keeper of the Northern Lights," the myth explains the origin of the northern lights while also teaching an important lesson. Write a multi-paragraph informational essay explaining the theme of the myth. Analyze how Lumara's actions, the conflict she faces, and the events of the story help develop the message about leadership, compassion, and helping others. Be sure to explain how the myth uses characters and events to communicate its deeper meaning. Support your response with evidence from the text. As you write your response: Use your own words to introduce and develop a topic; Use evidence from the passage to support your topic; and Use clear language to express ideas about your topic by making connections to your evidence Be sure to: Include an introduction; Create focused, organized paragraphs; Pay attention to the grammar, structure, and mechanics of your sentences; and Include a conclusion. Write your multi-paragraph response in the space provided.

passages.title: The Beaver and the Heron
passages.text:
(1) Along the edge of a wide river lived a hardworking beaver named Bram. Each day, Bram gathered branches, repaired his lodge, and strengthened the dam that protected his home. While other animals rested in the shade, Bram could usually be found carrying sticks through the water or inspecting his construction projects.

(2) Not far away lived a heron named Harlow. Harlow spent his days standing quietly near the riverbank, watching fish swim beneath the surface. He rarely hurried and often laughed at Bram's constant activity.

(3) "Why do you work so much?" Harlow asked one afternoon. "The sun is shining, the river is calm, and food is plentiful. You should spend more time relaxing."

(4) Bram placed another branch into the dam and smiled. "The river is calm today," he replied, "but conditions do not stay the same forever."

(5) Harlow shook his head. "You worry too much. There will always be time to prepare later."

(6) As summer continued, Bram worked steadily. He reinforced weak sections of the dam, stored extra food near his lodge, and cleared fallen branches from nearby channels. Whenever Harlow passed by, he teased the beaver about his endless projects.

(7) "You're missing all the fun," Harlow would say. "While you're working, I'm enjoying the beautiful weather."

(8) Bram simply nodded and continued his tasks.

(9) One evening, dark clouds gathered along the horizon. The wind began to howl through the trees, and distant thunder echoed across the valley. Animals hurried to find shelter as rain started to fall.

(10) The storm grew stronger throughout the night. Heavy rain poured into the river, causing water levels to rise rapidly. Powerful currents swept through the valley, carrying logs, branches, and debris downstream.

(11) Bram remained safely inside his lodge. His strengthened dam helped control the flow of water, and his stored food allowed him to stay sheltered during the storm.

(12) Harlow, however, faced a different situation. The riverbank where he usually rested flooded quickly. The shallow waters where he hunted fish became dangerous currents. With little shelter available, he struggled to stay safe as the storm continued.

(13) By morning, the rain had finally stopped. The river remained swollen, but the worst of the storm had passed. Animals cautiously emerged from their shelters to inspect the damage.

(14) Harlow flew across the valley searching for a place to rest. Eventually, he landed near Bram's lodge. The beaver was already busy repairing minor damage caused by the storm.

(15) "You were right," Harlow admitted. "I thought there would always be time to prepare later. I never expected the river to change so quickly."

(16) Bram paused his work and looked at the heron. "Preparing for challenges isn't about expecting the worst," he said. "It's about being ready when conditions change."

(17) Harlow thought about those words as he looked across the river. Many animals had spent the previous day enjoying the sunshine. Few had expected the storm to arrive so suddenly.

(18) Over the following weeks, Harlow began approaching life differently. He still enjoyed peaceful afternoons by the river, but he also spent time preparing for future challenges. He learned where to find shelter during storms and paid closer attention to changes in weather.

(19) Bram noticed the difference and smiled. The heron had not stopped enjoying life, but he had learned the value of balancing enjoyment with responsibility.

(20) From that day forward, whenever young animals asked Harlow for advice, he shared the lesson he had learned from the beaver. "Sunny days are wonderful," he would tell them, "but wise creatures prepare for rainy ones as well."

(21) And so the animals along the river remembered that planning ahead is not a sign of worry. It is a sign of wisdom.

questions.text:
Which theme is best developed throughout the fable?
A. Success comes from working harder than everyone else.
B. Preparation and responsibility help people face unexpected challenges.
C. Nature is impossible to predict or understand.
D. Enjoyment is more important than planning ahead.
Answer: B

How does the storm affect the interaction between Bram and Harlow?
A. It causes Bram to abandon his dam and leave the valley.
B. It proves that Harlow's approach is better than Bram's.
C. It shows Harlow the benefits of preparation and leads him to change his behavior.
D. It causes both animals to stop helping others.
Answer: C

Which sentence from the story best supports the inference that Harlow learned from his mistakes?
Harlow ________.
A. "rarely hurried and often laughed at Bram's constant activity" (paragraph 2)
B. "thought there would always be time to prepare later" (paragraph 15)
C. "began approaching life differently" and "learned where to find shelter during storms" (paragraph 18)
D. "still enjoyed peaceful afternoons by the river" (paragraph 18)
Answer: C

Which statement best summarizes the fable?
A. A beaver spends the summer building a dam while a heron enjoys relaxing by the river. After a storm damages the valley, the beaver repairs his lodge and continues working.
B. Bram works steadily to prepare for possible challenges, while Harlow believes preparation can wait. After a storm reveals the importance of being ready for change, Harlow adopts a more responsible approach and shares the lesson with others.
C. A storm floods a river valley and forces animals to search for new homes. Eventually, the animals rebuild their community together.
D. Harlow spends most of his time fishing while Bram builds dams. Although they disagree, they remain friends throughout the story.
Answer: B

writingPrompt.text: In "The Beaver and the Heron," the characters have different attitudes toward work and preparation. passages.title: The Legend of Thunder Ridge
passages.text:
(1) High above the Pine Valley stood a steep mountain known as Thunder Ridge. Its rocky cliffs rose above the surrounding forests, and during storms, thunder often echoed through the canyons with unusual intensity. For generations, local residents told a legend explaining why the mountain seemed to roar whenever dark clouds gathered.

(2) Long ago, according to the legend, a giant named Toren lived on the mountain. Toren was known for his incredible strength and his ability to shape stone with his bare hands. He spent his days carving pathways through the cliffs and creating shelters for travelers crossing the region.

(3) One year, a severe drought struck the valley. Rivers shrank, crops failed, and families worried about having enough water to survive. The people searched for solutions but could find none.

(4) Toren decided to seek help from the Sky Spirits, powerful beings believed to control the weather. Climbing to the highest peak, he called out for rain. The spirits listened but explained that a barrier of stone deep beneath the mountain prevented underground springs from reaching the valley.

(5) Determined to help, Toren struck the mountain with his enormous hammer. The first blow cracked the stone. The second blow opened a narrow passage. With the third blow, the barrier shattered completely.

(6) Water surged through the mountain and flowed into the valley below. Streams filled, crops recovered, and the people celebrated. The drought ended, and the valley prospered once again.

(7) According to the legend, Toren remained on Thunder Ridge to protect the water flowing through the mountain. Whenever storms approach, the sound of thunder is said to be Toren striking his hammer against the cliffs, ensuring the springs remain open for future generations.

(8) Today, visitors still hear thunder rolling through the mountains and remember the legend of the giant who used his strength to help others. Although historians do not consider the story factual, the legend continues to teach lessons about service, responsibility, and using one's abilities to benefit a community.

passages.title: The Legend of Silver Lake
passages.text:
(1) Several hundred miles away lies Silver Lake, a body of water known for its unusually reflective surface. On calm mornings, the lake mirrors the surrounding landscape so perfectly that it appears almost like a sheet of polished glass. Local traditions include a legend explaining how the lake gained its remarkable appearance.

(2) According to the story, a young woman named Alina lived in a village near the lake many generations ago. Alina was admired for her kindness and honesty. Whenever disagreements arose among neighbors, people often sought her advice because they trusted her judgment.

(3) One spring, tensions developed between villages located on opposite sides of the lake. Arguments over fishing rights and access to resources grew increasingly serious. As the disagreements continued, cooperation between the communities began to disappear.

(4) Concerned about the growing conflict, Alina traveled around the lake speaking with families from both villages. She listened carefully to their concerns and encouraged them to consider perspectives different from their own.

(5) Despite her efforts, neither side seemed willing to compromise. Frustrated by the lack of progress, Alina spent an evening sitting quietly beside the water. As the moon rose overhead, she noticed the lake reflecting both villages in a single image.

(6) Inspired by what she saw, Alina invited leaders from both communities to gather at the shore. She pointed toward the lake and asked them to look at their reflections.

(7) "The water does not choose one village over another," she said. "It reflects both equally. Perhaps we should learn from it."

(8) Her words encouraged the leaders to reconsider their positions. Over time, the villages negotiated agreements that allowed them to share resources and cooperate more effectively.

(9) According to the legend, the lake became unusually reflective as a reward for Alina's efforts to bring people together. Ever since then, its surface has served as a reminder that understanding often begins when people are willing to see situations from another person's perspective.

(10) Although the story cannot be verified through historical evidence, the legend remains an important part of local culture. Generations have shared Alina's story as a way of teaching values such as fairness, empathy, and cooperation.

questions.text:
How do the two legends use fictional elements to explain real places?
A. Both passages provide scientific explanations for natural features.
B. Both passages use fictional characters and events to explain the origins or significance of a local landmark.

Answer: B

C. Individuals can improve their communities by using their strengths to help others.

Answer: C

B. She became frustrated with the lack of progress. (paragraph 5)

D. She pointed out that the lake reflected both villages equally. (paragraph 7)
E. The lake was known for its reflective surface. (paragraph 1)
Answers: C, D

Which statement best compares the central events of the two legends?
A. Both stories describe leaders who use force to defeat enemies threatening their communities.
B. Toren solves a physical problem by breaking a barrier to release water, while Alina solves a social problem by helping two villages understand one another; both actions benefit their communities.
C. Both Toren and Alina discover magical objects that grant them special powers.
D. Toren and Alina both leave their homes in search of adventure and wealth.
Answer: B

title: The Princess and the Glass Garden

passages.title: The Princess and the Glass Garden
passages.text:
(1) Long ago, beyond seven hills and a silver river, stood a kingdom famous for its magnificent glass garden. The garden contained flowers crafted from crystal that sparkled in every color of the rainbow. According to legend, the flowers bloomed because of a magical pearl hidden beneath the palace.

(2) Princess Elara loved spending time among the crystal flowers. Unlike many royal children, she cared little for grand celebrations or expensive treasures. She preferred exploring the garden and learning about the people who lived throughout the kingdom.

(3) One winter, the crystal flowers suddenly lost their color. The roses turned gray, the lilies became dull, and the entire garden appeared lifeless. Soon afterward, the kingdom's rivers began shrinking and crops struggled to grow.

(4) The royal advisors searched for answers and eventually discovered that the magical pearl beneath the palace had disappeared. Without its magic, both the garden and the kingdom were weakening.

(5) Many knights volunteered to search for the pearl, but none returned with success. Some became lost in distant forests, while others followed false clues that led nowhere.

(6) Determined to help her people, Elara decided to begin her own journey. Before leaving, an elderly gardener handed her a small crystal seed.

(7) "This seed will only grow when someone chooses kindness over personal gain," the gardener explained.

(8) Elara traveled through forests, mountains, and valleys searching for clues. Along the way, she encountered travelers who needed assistance. She shared food with hungry strangers, helped repair a damaged bridge, and guided lost children back to their village.

(9) Each time Elara acted selflessly, the crystal seed glowed a little brighter.

(10) Eventually, she reached a hidden cave guarded by a giant raven. The bird revealed that the pearl had not been stolen. Instead, it had hidden itself because people throughout the kingdom had become increasingly selfish and focused on wealth.

(11) The raven explained that the pearl's magic could only return if someone proved that kindness still existed in the world.

(12) When Elara showed the glowing crystal seed, the cave filled with light. The pearl emerged from the shadows and floated into her hands.

(13) Upon returning home, Elara placed the pearl beneath the palace once more. The rivers began flowing, crops recovered, and the glass garden burst into brilliant color.

(14) From that day forward, the people remembered that the kingdom's greatest strength was not magic or wealth but compassion and generosity.

(15) And so the crystal flowers continued to bloom, reminding future generations that kindness often creates the most powerful magic of all.

passages.title: The Shoemaker and the Moonlit Cloak
passages.text:
(1) In a village nestled beside a dark forest lived a young shoemaker named Finn. Although he worked hard every day, he remained poor. His small shop barely earned enough money to cover his expenses.

(2) One evening, while walking home beneath a full moon, Finn discovered a silver cloak hanging from the branch of an ancient oak tree. The cloak shimmered with an unusual glow.

(3) As Finn examined it, a tiny fox with golden fur appeared beside him.

(4) "That cloak belongs to the Moon Weaver," the fox explained. "It has the power to grant one wish each night. But be careful—every wish reveals something about the person who makes it."

(5) Finn could hardly believe his luck. That night, he wished for enough leather to make more shoes. The next morning, stacks of fine leather appeared in his workshop.

(6) Over the following weeks, Finn used the cloak to help his business grow. Soon he had more customers than ever before.

(7) Then one evening, Finn overheard villagers discussing a problem. A recent storm had damaged several homes, and many families could not afford repairs before winter arrived.

(8) Finn returned home and stared at the glowing cloak. He could use the next wish to expand his business even further. Instead, he wished for building materials to help the struggling families.

(9) The following morning, lumber, tools, and supplies appeared throughout the village. Neighbors worked together to repair damaged homes before the first snowfall.

(10) As winter continued, Finn used additional wishes to help others. He provided blankets for families in need, repaired the village school, and supplied food during a difficult season.

(11) One moonlit evening, the golden fox returned.

(12) "The Moon Weaver has been watching," the fox said. "Most people use the cloak only for themselves. You chose differently."

(13) Suddenly, the silver cloak transformed into thousands of glowing threads that drifted into the sky like starlight.

(14) At first, Finn worried that the magic was gone. Then he noticed something remarkable. The village no longer needed magical wishes. The people had learned to help one another, and their cooperation had made the community stronger than any spell.

(15) From that day forward, Finn continued his work as a shoemaker. Though the magical cloak never returned, the kindness it inspired remained part of the village forever.

(16) And many years later, villagers still told stories about the shoemaker who discovered that the greatest gifts are often the ones we choose to share.

questions.text:
Which theme is developed in both passages?
A. Wealth is the most important source of happiness.
B. Kindness and generosity can create positive change for an entire community.
C. Magic should be kept hidden from others.
D. People should avoid helping others if it requires sacrifice.
Answer: B

How does a character's action help resolve the main problem in each story?
A. Elara finds the pearl by proving that kindness still exists, while Finn uses his wishes to help others, strengthening the village.
B. Elara defeats a giant raven, while Finn defeats the golden fox.
C. Elara searches distant kingdoms for treasure, while Finn uses the cloak to become wealthy.
D. Elara relies on magic alone, while Finn refuses to use the cloak.
Answer: A

Which detail best supports the inference that Finn changes from thinking about himself to thinking about others?
Finn first wished for ________, but later wished for ________.
A. a magical cloak; a larger shop
B. more customers; building materials to help struggling families
C. food for the village; a successful business
D. a repaired school; blankets for families
Answer: B

Which statement best compares the development of the central message in the two stories?
A. Both stories show characters using magic to gain power and become leaders.
B. Both stories show characters learning that wealth is less important than adventure.
C. Elara and Finn each make selfless choices that benefit others, and their actions lead to lasting improvements in their communities, reinforcing the idea that generosity has great power.
D. Elara and Finn both lose magical objects and spend years searching to recover them.
Answer: C

writingPrompt.text: Both "The Princess and the Glass Garden" and "The Shoemaker and the Moonlit Cloak" are fairy tales that feature magical objects and characters who face important choices. passages.title: The Mystery of Locker 317
passages.text:
(1) Everyone at Maple Ridge Middle School knew about Locker 317. It sat at the end of the seventh-grade hallway between the science lab and the art room. The locker looked completely ordinary except for one detail: every Monday morning, a different object appeared inside it. No one knew who placed the items there, and no one had ever been seen opening the locker.

(2) Over the years, students had discovered all kinds of strange things inside. One week it held a compass with no markings. Another week it contained a handwritten map of the school from decades earlier. There had even been an old photograph showing the town's main street before any of the current buildings existed. Each object sparked rumors, but no explanation ever surfaced.

(3) Most students treated the mystery as a joke. Some believed teachers secretly placed the items there. Others claimed the locker was haunted. Fourteen-year-old Ava Carter wasn't interested in the rumors. She preferred facts to stories. Still, she couldn't help becoming curious when a small brass key appeared in the locker one Monday morning.

(4) The key was attached to a tag labeled simply: Find the beginning. Within hours, photos of the key spread across social media. Students gathered around the locker between classes, offering theories about what the message meant.

(5) Ava studied the key carefully. Unlike many of her classmates, she noticed details others ignored. The tag appeared old, and the brass key showed signs of wear. Most importantly, a tiny number was engraved near the handle: 1928.

(6) That afternoon, Ava visited the school library. She searched through yearbooks, newspaper clippings, and local history records. Eventually, she discovered that Maple Ridge Middle School had been built in 1928. The date on the key matched the year the school opened.

(7) Excited by the discovery, Ava continued researching. Buried inside an old newspaper article, she found a photograph of the school's original cornerstone ceremony. Standing near the entrance was a metal time capsule scheduled to be opened fifty years later. Yet Ava could find no record showing that the capsule had ever been opened.

(8) The next day, she shared her findings with her friend Ben. At first, he laughed. "You think the key belongs to a time capsule?"

(9) "Maybe," Ava replied. "Or maybe it belongs to something connected to the time capsule. Either way, the date can't be a coincidence."

(10) After school, the two friends examined the front entrance of the building. Near one corner, partially hidden behind a row of shrubs, they discovered a weathered plaque marking the original cornerstone. Beneath it was a small metal panel with a keyhole.

(11) Ava's heart raced. She inserted the brass key and turned it carefully. With a soft click, the panel opened. Inside they found a narrow compartment containing a sealed envelope.

(12) The envelope was addressed To the Students of Maple Ridge. Unsure what to do next, Ava and Ben brought it to the principal. Soon several teachers gathered to examine the discovery.

(13) Inside the envelope was a letter written by students nearly one hundred years earlier. The letter described their hopes for future generations. They wrote about wanting students to value learning, work together, and take pride in their community. At the end of the letter was a final message: Every school has a story. Keep discovering yours.

(14) News of the discovery spread quickly. Students who had never cared about local history suddenly wanted to learn more about the school's past. Teachers organized projects exploring the community's history, and the library created a display featuring photographs and artifacts from previous decades.

(15) A week later, Locker 317 revealed another object—a small notebook labeled Chapter Two. This time, however, the mystery felt different. Instead of focusing on wild rumors, students became interested in solving the puzzle together.

(16) Ava never learned who originally placed the key inside the locker. Yet she realized that the identity of the person mattered less than the lesson they had inspired. Sometimes curiosity could connect people to stories they never knew existed.

(17) As she walked past Locker 317 one afternoon, Ava noticed a crowd gathering around the newest clue. She smiled and continued toward class. The mystery was far from solved, but that was part of what made it exciting.

(18) After all, every good story begins with a question—and sometimes the best discoveries happen when someone decides to look for the answer.

questions.text:

A. The oldest traditions should never be questioned.
B. Curiosity and investigation can lead to meaningful discoveries.
C. Technology is more reliable than historical records.

Answer: B

Which statement best summarizes the story?

B. Students at Maple Ridge become interested in a mysterious locker after a key appears inside. By researching the school's history, Ava and Ben uncover a hidden letter from students of the past, inspiring the school community to learn more about its history and continue exploring the mystery.
C. Ben and Ava compete to solve a mystery, but neither discovers who placed the objects in Locker 317.
D. A principal creates a history project after students become distracted by rumors about a haunted locker.
Answer: B

passages.title: The Observatory
passages.text:
(1) The observatory sat alone at the top of Briar Hill, where the town lights seemed distant and small. Its white dome rose above the surrounding trees like a silent moon resting on the earth. During the day, visitors often overlooked the building. They passed it on hiking trails or spotted it from the road below without giving it much thought. At night, however, the observatory became something entirely different.

(2) As darkness settled over the landscape, the familiar world seemed to change. The noise of traffic faded. The outlines of houses and streets softened. Above the hill, the sky expanded into a vast canvas of stars. Constellations emerged one by one, forming patterns that had guided travelers, sailors, and explorers for centuries. The farther the daylight retreated, the more the heavens revealed.

(3) Inside the observatory, a large telescope pointed toward the opening in the dome. The machine's metal frame gleamed beneath dim lights. Nearby shelves held star charts, astronomy books, and photographs of distant galaxies. Everything appeared still, as though the room itself were waiting.

(4) Through the telescope's lens, familiar stars transformed into brilliant points of light. Some appeared blue, others white or golden. Planets that looked no different from stars to the naked eye suddenly revealed details of their own. Rings surrounded Saturn. Bands of clouds stretched across Jupiter. Tiny moons appeared beside distant worlds like silent companions.

(5) Visitors often arrived expecting to see something dramatic—a meteor shower, a comet, or a spectacular cosmic event. Yet many found themselves most affected by something simpler. Standing beneath the night sky created a feeling difficult to describe. The stars seemed both impossibly distant and strangely connected to life on Earth.

(6) Along one wall hung photographs taken through the observatory's telescope. Spiral galaxies stretched across the images like glowing whirlpools. Nebulas appeared as clouds of color and light drifting through space. Some photographs captured objects so far away that their light had begun its journey toward Earth long before modern cities existed.

(7) Looking at those images often changed the way visitors viewed time. Daily concerns that seemed important during the afternoon felt smaller beneath the stars. School assignments, busy schedules, and minor frustrations faded into the background. In their place came a sense of perspective. The universe was vast beyond imagination, yet every person occupied a unique place within it.

(8) The observatory's guestbook contained comments from visitors collected over many years. Some wrote about scientific discoveries they hoped to make one day. Others described memories shared with family members during stargazing events. Many entries expressed the same idea in different ways: the night sky inspired wonder.

(9) Scientists often explain the stars through mathematics, physics, and observation. They measure distances, analyze light, and study patterns. Yet even with all that knowledge, the sky continues to inspire questions. What else exists beyond the galaxies that have already been discovered? How many planets orbit distant stars? Could life exist elsewhere in the universe?

(10) The observatory did not provide answers to all those questions. Instead, it offered something different—a place where curiosity could grow. Every observation seemed to lead to another question. Every discovery revealed something new waiting to be explored.

(11) Near midnight, the dome slowly rotated, following the movement of the stars across the sky. Outside, the town below remained quiet. Porch lights glowed faintly in the distance, and the streets appeared almost empty. Above them stretched the same sky that generations before had studied, admired, and wondered about.

(12) The stars themselves seemed unchanged. They had shone over ancient civilizations, early explorers, inventors, and dreamers. They continued shining over students, scientists, and future generations who would someday ask their own questions about the universe.

(13) Standing inside the observatory, it was easy to understand why people returned again and again. The building was more than a place for studying astronomy. It was a reminder that curiosity has always been part of the human experience. Long after visitors left the hill and returned to their daily routines, the stars remained overhead, inviting them to keep looking upward.

questions.text:
How does the setting of the observatory contribute to the ideas developed in the passage?
A. The isolated hilltop location helps visitors focus on the night sky and experience a sense of wonder and curiosity.
B. The observatory's location makes it difficult for scientists to study the stars.
C. The busy town below distracts visitors from learning about astronomy.
D. The observatory's daytime appearance encourages visitors to explore the universe.
Answer: A

Which central idea is developed throughout the passage?
A. Scientific discoveries have answered most questions about the universe.
B. The night sky inspires curiosity and encourages people to explore questions beyond their everyday experiences.
C. Astronomy is only meaningful to professional scientists.
D. Modern technology has made stargazing less important than it once was.
Answer: B

Which TWO details best support the idea that the observatory encourages curiosity rather than simply providing answers?

B. The observatory's guestbook contained comments from visitors. (paragraph 8)
C. Scientists continue to ask questions about distant galaxies, planets, and life elsewhere in the universe. (paragraph 9)
D. Every observation seemed to lead to another question, and every discovery revealed something new waiting to be explored. (paragraph 10)
E. The dome slowly rotated near midnight. (paragraph 11)
Answers: C, D

Which statement best summarizes the passage?
A. The passage explains how telescopes work and describes several objects that can be observed in space.
B. The passage describes a hilltop observatory and the sights visible through its telescope. It explains how observing the night sky gives people perspective, inspires wonder, and encourages ongoing curiosity about the universe.
C. The passage argues that astronomy is more important than other scientific fields because it studies distant galaxies.
D. The passage focuses on the history of one observatory and the scientists who worked there.
Answer: B

writingPrompt.text: In "The Observatory," the author uses description and reflection to develop a vivid scene and convey deeper ideas. Support your response with evidence from the text. passages.title: The Train Station at Dawn
passages.text:
(1) The train station seemed suspended between yesterday and tomorrow. Beneath the pale light of dawn, the brick building stood quietly at the edge of the city, its tall clock tower watching over empty benches and silent tracks. The first hints of sunlight stretched across the horizon, painting the sky in shades of gold, pink, and silver.

(2) Inside the station, the air carried a mixture of scents—fresh coffee from a small café, worn leather from travelers' luggage, and the faint metallic smell of the tracks outside. The polished floor reflected the soft glow of overhead lights that had not yet surrendered to the morning.

(3) A few passengers sat scattered throughout the waiting area. Some stared at their phones while others watched the large departure board mounted above the ticket counters. A woman turned the pages of a novel. A businessman adjusted his tie and glanced repeatedly at his watch. Nearby, a child pressed both hands against the window, searching for the first train of the day.

(4) Every few minutes, a voice echoed through the station announcing arrivals and departures. The sound bounced off the high ceilings before fading into silence. The announcements seemed to remind everyone that movement was coming, even though the station remained still for the moment.

(5) Outside, rows of steel tracks stretched toward distant cities. They disappeared into the morning haze, suggesting destinations beyond what the eye could see. The tracks seemed less like pieces of transportation and more like pathways connecting thousands of stories.

(6) The station existed as a place of transition. Some travelers were beginning adventures. Others were returning home. A few might never visit the city again. Yet for a brief period, their paths crossed beneath the same roof.

(7) As the first train approached, a low rumble traveled through the rails. Conversations grew quieter. Passengers gathered their belongings. The stillness that had filled the station all morning slowly gave way to movement.

(8) Moments later, doors opened, passengers boarded, and the train disappeared into the distance. The station returned to its quiet rhythm, ready to welcome the next group of travelers carrying destinations, memories, and expectations of their own.

passages.title: The Rooftop Garden
passages.text:
(1) Seven stories above the crowded streets, the rooftop garden seemed like a different world. Below, traffic moved through intersections, buses stopped to collect passengers, and people hurried along sidewalks. Above, the garden offered a rare pocket of calm surrounded by concrete and glass.

(2) Raised flower beds lined narrow pathways that wound across the rooftop. Bright marigolds, lavender, and sunflowers swayed gently in the breeze. Small vegetable plants filled wooden containers, their leaves reaching toward the sunlight. Bees drifted from flower to flower, largely unaware of the city stretching beneath them.

(3) A wooden bench sat near the garden's edge. From there, visitors could see rooftops, office buildings, and distant bridges crossing the river. The city skyline formed a jagged silhouette against the afternoon sky.

(4) Unlike parks at ground level, the rooftop garden felt hidden. The sounds of the city arrived softened and distant. Sirens became faint echoes. Conversations blended into an indistinct hum. The separation created a sense of quiet that seemed unusual in such a busy place.

(5) Several gardeners moved slowly among the plants. One carefully watered tomato vines climbing a trellis. Another removed weeds from a flower bed. Their movements were unhurried, matching the peaceful atmosphere around them.

(6) Along one wall, painted murals displayed scenes of forests, rivers, and mountains. The artwork blended with the living plants, creating the illusion that nature and city life occupied the same space. The contrast between steel buildings and growing flowers made both seem more noticeable.

(7) As the afternoon progressed, sunlight shifted across the rooftop. Shadows lengthened, and the colors of the flowers deepened. The garden remained unchanged by the rush of activity below, offering a place where people could pause, observe, and reflect.

(8) For many visitors, the rooftop garden was more than a collection of plants. It served as a reminder that even in the busiest environments, spaces for quiet, beauty, and connection can still be found.

questions.text:
How does the setting contribute to the development of ideas in both passages?
A. Both settings create dangerous situations that characters must overcome.
B. Both settings serve as places where people can pause, reflect, and think about experiences beyond their daily routines.

C. Some travelers were beginning adventures while others were returning home. (paragraph 6)

Which theme is best developed in The Rooftop Garden?
A. Nature is more important than cities.
B. People should avoid busy environments whenever possible.
C. Places of peace and beauty can exist even in busy, crowded environments.
D. Gardening is the most rewarding hobby.
Answer: C

Which statement best compares the passages?
A. Both passages describe locations that offer visitors more than their physical appearance. The train station represents journeys and new possibilities, while the rooftop garden provides a peaceful place for reflection within a busy city.
B. Both passages focus on the history of famous landmarks and explain how they were constructed.
C. The train station emphasizes quiet and isolation, while the rooftop garden focuses on competition and activity.
D. Both passages argue that cities should replace public transportation with green spaces.
Answer: A

passages.title: The Last Shot
passages.text:
(1) The gymnasium was nearly empty except for the sound of a basketball bouncing against the hardwood floor. Emma glanced at the scoreboard above the bleachers. Five minutes remained before tryouts officially ended, and she still wasn't sure she belonged there.

(2) Most of the other players had spent years on travel teams and recreation leagues. Emma had only started playing basketball the previous year. While her classmates talked about tournaments and championships, she was still learning advanced plays and strategies.

(3) During drills, she worked hard but made several mistakes. One pass sailed out of bounds. Another time, she missed an easy layup. Each error seemed larger than the last.

(4) As tryouts continued, Emma noticed the coach writing notes on a clipboard. Every time he glanced in her direction, she felt more nervous. By the final scrimmage, she was convinced she had no chance of making the team.

(5) With less than a minute remaining, Emma's team trailed by one point. The coach blew the whistle and signaled for play to continue. The players raced across the court.

(6) Emma received a pass near the corner. For a split second, she considered passing the ball to someone else. Then she remembered all the hours she had spent practicing after school.

(7) She took a deep breath and shot.

(8) The ball struck the backboard, bounced off the rim, and dropped through the net.

(9) Her teammates cheered as the buzzer sounded. Although the basket won the scrimmage, Emma wasn't sure it would matter. One successful shot couldn't erase all her mistakes.

(10) Two days later, the team roster was posted outside the gym. Emma hesitated before reading it. Then she saw her name.

(11) Later that afternoon, she asked the coach why she had been selected.

(12) "You weren't the most experienced player," he admitted. "But every time something went wrong, you kept working. Skills can improve. Effort and determination are harder to teach."

(13) Walking home, Emma realized the tryout had been about more than basketball. Success often depended not on being perfect but on refusing to quit after making mistakes.

passages.title: The Broken Robot
passages.text:
(1) Lucas stared at the robot sitting motionless on the workbench. After six weeks of preparation, coding, and testing, the machine had stopped working the day before the regional robotics competition.

(2) He and his teammates had spent countless afternoons building the robot. It could sort objects, navigate obstacles, and complete tasks with impressive accuracy. At least it could before the malfunction appeared.

(3) The problem seemed impossible to find. Every component looked normal. The batteries worked. The sensors responded correctly. Yet the robot refused to move.

(4) Several teammates suggested starting over with a simplified design. Others argued that there wasn't enough time. Frustration filled the room as the competition approached.

(5) Lucas understood why everyone felt discouraged. He felt the same way. Still, he couldn't stop thinking about the problem.

(6) That evening, he reviewed weeks of test data and design notes. Hours passed as he examined diagrams and computer logs. Just before midnight, he noticed something unusual.

(7) A tiny programming update installed three days earlier had changed how the robot interpreted sensor information. The update wasn't causing a major failure. Instead, it was creating a small error that prevented the robot from completing its startup sequence.

(8) The next morning, Lucas explained his discovery to the team. Together they adjusted the code and restarted the robot.

(9) The machine rolled forward.

(10) Cheers erupted throughout the workshop.

(11) At the competition, the robot performed well and earned second place. Although the team didn't win the championship, they were proud of what they had accomplished.

(12) Looking back, Lucas realized the most important lesson had come from the setback itself. If the robot had worked perfectly from the beginning, the team never would have learned how much persistence and careful problem-solving mattered.

(13) Sometimes obstacles weren't signs that a goal should be abandoned. Sometimes they were opportunities to learn how to overcome challenges.

Short Stories: complete narratives in brief format

questions.text:

A. Winning is more important than learning from mistakes.
B. Success often comes from persistence and continuing to work through challenges.

Answer: B

C. Emma gains confidence to take an important shot, while Lucas continues investigating the robot's malfunction until he finds a solution.

Answer: C

B. "Hours passed as he examined diagrams and computer logs." (paragraph 6)

Answer: D

B. Emma and Lucas both win championships because they are the most talented participants.
C. Emma struggles with basketball while Lucas struggles with robotics, and both eventually decide to pursue different interests.
D. Emma succeeds because of one lucky shot, while Lucas succeeds because of help from his teammates.
Answer: A

title: Ryan Trahan: Turning Creativity into Opportunity

passages.title: Ryan Trahan: Turning Creativity into Opportunity
passages.text:
(1) Millions of people know Ryan Trahan as a YouTuber who creates entertaining challenges, travel adventures, and fundraising campaigns. His videos often feature unusual ideas, such as crossing the United States with only a few dollars or completing creative challenges with limited resources. While many viewers see the excitement and humor in his content, Ryan's success is rooted in determination, creativity, and a willingness to take risks.

(2) Ryan Michael Trahan was born on October 7, 1998, in Eagle Lake, Texas. As a student, he enjoyed sports and competed in cross-country and track. Running became a major part of his life, and he worked hard to improve his performance. His dedication eventually earned him a scholarship to Texas A&M University, where he planned to continue competing as a collegiate athlete.

(3) While attending college, Ryan became interested in creating online content. At the time, YouTube was already home to many popular creators, but Ryan believed he could contribute something unique. He began producing videos that combined humor, storytelling, and personal experiences. Building an audience was not easy. Like many creators, he spent hours planning, filming, editing, and posting videos without knowing whether people would watch them.

(4) Eventually, Ryan faced an important decision. Changes to athletic eligibility rules created challenges that affected both his college athletics and his growing online career. After careful consideration, he decided to leave college and focus on content creation full time. The choice involved significant uncertainty. Success on social media is never guaranteed, and many creators struggle to build sustainable careers.

(5) Ryan approached the challenge with the same determination he had shown as an athlete. He continued experimenting with different video formats and ideas. Rather than copying trends, he focused on creating content that reflected his own personality and interests. His willingness to try new approaches helped him stand out in a highly competitive environment.

(6) One of Ryan's most successful qualities is his ability to transform simple ideas into engaging stories. A challenge that might seem ordinary at first often becomes an opportunity to explore problem-solving, perseverance, and creativity. Viewers are not only interested in the final result but also in the process of overcoming obstacles along the way.

(7) In recent years, Ryan gained widespread attention for a series of fundraising campaigns that combined entertainment with charitable giving. During one project, he traveled across the United States while raising money for organizations that provided access to clean drinking water. The campaign attracted millions of viewers and generated substantial donations. Similar projects demonstrated how social media platforms can be used not only for entertainment but also to support meaningful causes.

(8) Ryan frequently emphasizes the importance of persistence. Many of his videos include moments when plans do not go as expected. Transportation problems, unexpected expenses, weather conditions, and other obstacles often create setbacks. Instead of ending the project, however, he adapts and continues moving forward. These experiences help illustrate that challenges are a natural part of pursuing ambitious goals.

(9) Another factor contributing to Ryan's success is his focus on storytelling. Effective storytelling allows creators to connect with audiences by making experiences feel personal and relatable. Whether he is documenting a journey, completing a challenge, or raising money for charity, Ryan organizes his content around a clear narrative that keeps viewers engaged.

(10) Beyond YouTube, Ryan has become an entrepreneur and business owner. He has launched companies, collaborated with other creators, and explored new ways of reaching audiences. These efforts demonstrate that content creation often involves skills beyond filming videos. Successful creators frequently need to understand marketing, communication, budgeting, and business management.

(11) Ryan's career reflects larger changes in how people share information and entertainment. In previous generations, individuals often needed access to television networks, publishing companies, or major studios to reach large audiences. Today, digital platforms allow creators to connect directly with viewers around the world.

(12) Despite his success, Ryan's journey did not follow a predictable path. He experienced uncertainty, took calculated risks, and invested significant effort into developing his skills. His story demonstrates that achievement often results from consistent work over time rather than immediate success.

(13) For many young people, Ryan Trahan serves as an example of how creativity can be combined with perseverance to create opportunities. His experiences show that innovative ideas, strong communication skills, and a willingness to learn can help individuals achieve goals in fields that did not even exist a generation ago.

(14) As technology continues to evolve, new careers and opportunities will emerge. Ryan's story reminds people that success often belongs to those who are willing to adapt, experiment, and continue moving forward when faced with challenges.

questions.text:

A. Success is usually achieved through luck rather than effort.
B. Taking risks is never worthwhile because outcomes are uncertain.
C. Creativity, perseverance, and adaptability can help people achieve meaningful goals.

Answer: C

Answer: B

passages.title: Caitlin Clark: Changing the Game
passages.text:
(1) Long before Caitlin Clark became one of the most recognized names in basketball, she was a young athlete growing up in West Des Moines, Iowa. From an early age, she loved competition and spent countless hours practicing sports. Whether she was shooting baskets, playing softball, or competing against older players, Clark constantly looked for ways to improve.

(2) Basketball quickly became her greatest passion. While many young athletes focus on having fun, Clark also developed a strong desire to challenge herself. She often played against older and more experienced competitors, believing that difficult competition would help her become a better player.

(3) As she advanced through middle school and high school, Clark's talent became impossible to ignore. She attended Dowling Catholic High School, where she earned recognition as one of the nation's top basketball players. Her ability to score from long distances, make difficult passes, and lead her team attracted attention from colleges across the country.

(4) Despite receiving opportunities from many well-known basketball programs, Clark chose to attend the University of Iowa. The decision surprised some observers because larger universities often receive more national attention. Clark, however, believed Iowa was the right place to continue developing as both a student and an athlete.

(5) During her first college season, Clark immediately demonstrated her abilities. She scored points at an impressive rate and regularly created opportunities for teammates. Her combination of shooting, passing, and leadership helped Iowa become one of the most exciting teams in college basketball.

(6) As her career progressed, Clark became known for making shots from distances that many players would never attempt. Fans filled arenas hoping to watch her compete, and television audiences grew whenever Iowa played. Her style of play brought new attention to women's basketball and introduced the sport to many viewers who had rarely watched it before.

(7) Success did not come without challenges. Opposing teams often designed entire defensive strategies to stop her. Every game brought intense pressure, high expectations, and constant scrutiny from fans and media. Clark responded by continuing to work on her skills and focusing on helping her team improve.

(8) One of the most remarkable aspects of Clark's career was her ability to inspire others. Young athletes across the country began practicing basketball after watching her play. Many admired not only her talent but also her confidence and willingness to take risks during important moments.

(9) During her time at Iowa, Clark broke numerous records, including scoring records that had stood for decades. Each achievement generated headlines, but Clark often emphasized the importance of teamwork. She regularly credited coaches, teammates, and family members for supporting her development.

(10) In addition to her accomplishments on the court, Clark helped increase interest in women's sports more broadly. Attendance records were broken at many games, merchandise sales increased, and television ratings reached new heights. Sports analysts noted that her popularity demonstrated the growing audience for women's athletics.

(11) Clark's influence extended beyond basketball statistics. She became an example of how dedication and preparation can help individuals achieve ambitious goals. Her story showed that success often results from years of consistent effort rather than a single moment of achievement.

(12) After completing her college career, Clark entered professional basketball. Expectations remained high, but she approached the next stage of her career the same way she had approached every challenge before—with preparation, determination, and a willingness to keep improving.

(13) Many athletes dream of reaching the highest levels of competition. What makes Clark's story particularly notable is the impact she had on the sport itself. By attracting new fans and inspiring young players, she helped shape the future of basketball.

(14) Today, Caitlin Clark is recognized not only for her records and accomplishments but also for the influence she has had on athletes and fans. Her journey demonstrates how hard work, resilience, and confidence can help a person achieve success while creating opportunities for others along the way.

questions.text:

Answer: B

Answer: C

Answer: A

A. The author presents Clark as a controversial figure whose success depended mainly on luck.
B. The author uses an objective, informative point of view to highlight both Clark's achievements and her broader influence on basketball.
C. The author focuses only on Clark's personal opinions about basketball.
D. The author compares Clark directly to other athletes to criticize her accomplishments.
Answer: B

passages.title: MrBeast: Redefining Online Entertainment
passages.text:
(1) Millions of people around the world know Jimmy Donaldson by his online name, MrBeast. Through videos that combine large-scale challenges, philanthropy, and entertainment, he has become one of the most influential content creators on the internet. Although his videos often feature enormous prizes and ambitious projects, his success began with a simple interest in creating online content.

(2) Donaldson was born in Wichita, Kansas, in 1998 and grew up in North Carolina. As a teenager, he became fascinated by YouTube and spent countless hours studying successful videos. While many viewers simply watched content for entertainment, Donaldson analyzed what made videos popular and how creators connected with audiences.

(3) In 2012, at the age of thirteen, he launched his own YouTube channel. His early videos attracted relatively few viewers, and growth came slowly. Despite limited success, he continued experimenting with different formats and ideas. Many of his earliest projects required significant effort but generated little attention.

(4) Over time, Donaldson discovered that audiences enjoyed unusual challenges and creative concepts. As his channel expanded, he began producing increasingly ambitious videos. Some involved endurance challenges, while others featured elaborate competitions with large prizes.

(5) What separated MrBeast from many other creators was his willingness to reinvest resources into future projects. Instead of focusing solely on personal profit, he often used earnings to create larger videos or support charitable efforts. This approach helped his content stand out in a crowded digital landscape.

(6) As his audience grew into the millions, Donaldson launched projects designed to address real-world problems. He organized fundraising campaigns, supported food banks, funded environmental initiatives, and contributed to efforts aimed at improving access to clean water. These activities demonstrated that social media platforms could be used for purposes beyond entertainment.

(7) Success also brought challenges. Producing large-scale videos required extensive planning, coordination, and financial management. Each project involved risk because there was no guarantee that viewers would respond positively. Nevertheless, Donaldson continued experimenting with new ideas and adapting to changing trends.

(8) Today, MrBeast's influence extends far beyond YouTube. His work has inspired other creators, attracted international audiences, and demonstrated how creativity and entrepreneurship can be combined to reach millions of people. His career illustrates the impact that innovation, persistence, and strategic thinking can have in the digital age.

passages.title: Simone Biles: Reaching New Heights
passages.text:
(1) Simone Biles is widely considered one of the greatest gymnasts in history. Her accomplishments include multiple Olympic medals, world championships, and skills so difficult that several have been named after her. Yet her path to success involved overcoming obstacles both inside and outside the gym.

(2) Biles was born in Columbus, Ohio, in 1997. During her early childhood, she faced challenges that eventually led to her being raised by her grandparents, who later adopted her. Their support provided the stability that allowed her to pursue her interests and develop her talents.

(3) Biles first became interested in gymnastics during a field trip. Coaches quickly recognized her natural athletic ability and encouraged her to train more seriously. As her skills improved, she devoted increasing amounts of time to practice and competition.

(4) Gymnastics requires extraordinary strength, flexibility, coordination, and discipline. Biles spent years refining her techniques and mastering increasingly difficult routines. Progress often came through repetition, patience, and a willingness to learn from mistakes.

(5) Her dedication produced remarkable results. Biles won national and international competitions while developing skills that many experts considered groundbreaking. She became known for performing routines with a level of difficulty rarely seen in the sport.

(6) In addition to her athletic achievements, Biles has spoken openly about the importance of mental health. During the 2020 Olympic Games, she made the difficult decision to step back from several events to prioritize her well-being. Her decision sparked international discussions about the pressures faced by elite athletes.

(7) By sharing her experiences, Biles helped encourage conversations about resilience, self-awareness, and the importance of seeking support when facing challenges. Many people admired her willingness to make difficult decisions despite intense public attention.

(8) Today, Simone Biles continues to inspire athletes around the world. Her story demonstrates that success involves more than winning competitions. It also includes perseverance, courage, and the ability to make choices that support long-term well-being.

D. The author argues that one person was more successful than the other.

passages.title: Mark Rober: Engineering Through Creativity
passages.text:
(1) Millions of people know Mark Rober as a science communicator and YouTube creator who uses engineering to solve unusual problems. His videos often feature creative inventions, experiments, and challenges that make science accessible to broad audiences. Before becoming an online creator, however, Rober spent years working as an engineer.

(2) Mark Rober grew up with a strong curiosity about how things worked. As a child, he enjoyed building projects, experimenting with ideas, and finding creative solutions to everyday problems. These interests eventually led him to study engineering in college.

(3) After completing his education, Rober began working for NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory. During his time there, he contributed to projects involving robotic exploration of Mars. Engineers working on such missions must solve complex problems because equipment needs to function reliably in extreme conditions millions of miles from Earth.

(4) Working on space exploration required technical knowledge, teamwork, and persistence. Engineers frequently encounter unexpected challenges that require careful testing and revision. Rober later explained that solving problems often involves learning from failures and continuously improving designs.

(5) After several years at NASA, Rober worked as an engineer for a technology company before deciding to focus more heavily on science communication. He realized that many people found science intimidating or difficult to understand. Through videos and demonstrations, he hoped to show that science could be both educational and entertaining.

(6) Many of Rober's projects involve building devices that demonstrate scientific principles. Some experiments investigate physics, while others focus on engineering design or environmental issues. His ability to explain complicated concepts in simple ways has helped millions of viewers better understand science.

(7) One of Rober's most notable qualities is his emphasis on curiosity. Rather than presenting science as a collection of facts to memorize, he encourages people to ask questions, test ideas, and explore possible solutions. His work highlights the importance of creativity in scientific thinking.

(8) Today, Mark Rober continues to inspire students, educators, and aspiring engineers. His career demonstrates that curiosity, persistence, and a willingness to experiment can lead to innovation and discovery.

passages.title: Temple Grandin: Seeing Problems Differently
passages.text:
(1) Temple Grandin is a scientist, inventor, and advocate whose ideas have influenced agriculture, animal welfare, and education. Throughout her career, she has used her unique way of thinking to solve problems and improve systems used around the world.

(2) Grandin was born in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1947. As a young child, she faced challenges related to autism spectrum disorder. At a time when many people understood very little about autism, some experts doubted that she would succeed in school or develop professional skills.

(3) Despite these obstacles, Grandin's family and teachers encouraged her interests and strengths. She developed a strong ability to think visually, often describing her thought process as creating detailed mental images. This perspective helped her approach problems differently from many other people.

(4) As she pursued higher education, Grandin became interested in animal behavior. She observed how livestock responded to their surroundings and noticed that certain farming practices caused unnecessary stress. Using her observations, she designed systems that allowed animals to move more calmly and safely.

(5) Many of Grandin's innovations were based on her ability to view situations from perspectives others had overlooked. By carefully observing details, she identified practical solutions that improved conditions for animals while also helping farmers work more efficiently.

(6) In addition to her scientific contributions, Grandin became a public speaker and author. She has written extensively about autism, education, and problem-solving. Through books, presentations, and interviews, she has helped people better understand neurodiversity and the value of different ways of thinking.

(7) Grandin often emphasizes that innovation can come from individuals who approach challenges differently. She encourages students to develop their strengths, remain curious, and persist when facing difficulties.

(8) Today, Temple Grandin is recognized as an influential scientist and advocate. Her life demonstrates how determination, creativity, and unique perspectives can lead to meaningful contributions that improve the lives of others.

questions.text:
Which theme is developed across both passages?
A. Innovation often results from curiosity, perseverance, and a willingness to think in new ways.
B. Success depends primarily on natural talent.
C. People should avoid challenges that seem difficult.
D. Scientific discoveries are only possible through teamwork.
Answer: A

Which two details best support the idea that both Mark Rober and Temple Grandin used unique approaches to solve problems?
A. Rober emphasizes asking questions, testing ideas, and exploring possible solutions. (Passage 1, paragraph 7)
B. Rober worked at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory. (Passage 1, paragraph 3)
C. Grandin identified practical solutions by observing details that others overlooked. (Passage 2, paragraph 5)
D. Grandin was born in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1947. (Passage 2, paragraph 2)
Answers: A and C

Complete the summary.
Both passages describe individuals who used creativity and determination to make meaningful contributions. Mark Rober applies engineering and science communication to help others understand complex ideas, while Temple Grandin developed innovative solutions by approaching problems from a unique perspective. Together, the passages show that progress often comes from (thinking creatively and persisting through challenges / following the same methods as everyone else / avoiding difficult problems).
Answer: thinking creatively and persisting through challenges

How does the author's point of view contribute to both passages?
A. The author presents an objective account that highlights how each person's experiences, choices, and perseverance led to positive contributions.
B. The author tells the stories from the first-person perspective of Mark Rober and Temple Grandin.
C. The author argues that one person's achievements are more important than the other's.
D. The author focuses only on challenges and ignores accomplishments.
Answer: A

title: The Race to the South Pole

passages.title: The Race to the South Pole
passages.text:
(1) In the early 1900s, much of Antarctica remained unexplored. Maps showed vast blank spaces, and scientists knew little about the continent's interior. The South Pole, located at the southernmost point on Earth, had never been reached by humans. For explorers around the world, reaching the pole became one of the greatest geographic challenges of the era.

(2) Two expedition leaders emerged as the primary competitors in what became known as the Race to the South Pole. Roald Amundsen of Norway and Robert Falcon Scott of Great Britain both organized teams with the goal of becoming the first people to reach the pole. Although they shared the same objective, their approaches differed in important ways.

(3) Amundsen had extensive experience traveling in polar regions. He carefully studied survival techniques used by Indigenous peoples living in Arctic environments. His team relied heavily on skis and dog sleds, methods that had proven effective in extreme cold. Amundsen believed that efficiency and preparation would be critical to success.

(4) Scott, a British naval officer and explorer, also prepared extensively for the expedition. His team used a combination of motorized sleds, ponies, dogs, and human effort to transport supplies. In addition to reaching the South Pole, Scott's expedition included scientific objectives. Team members collected geological samples and conducted research throughout their journey.

(5) In 1911, both expeditions established base camps on the Antarctic coast and began preparing for the long trek inland. The environment presented enormous challenges. Temperatures frequently dropped far below freezing, powerful winds swept across the ice, and crevasses hidden beneath snow created constant hazards.

(6) Amundsen's team departed first. Traveling efficiently with dog sleds and skis, the explorers crossed glaciers and climbed mountain ranges while carrying supplies needed for the return trip. Along the route, they established supply depots that would help sustain them during the journey home.

(7) Scott's team followed a different route toward the pole. Progress proved slower than expected. Mechanical equipment experienced difficulties, and some transportation methods were less effective in Antarctic conditions than planners had hoped. Nevertheless, the team continued advancing across the ice.

(8) On December 14, 1911, Amundsen and his team reached the South Pole. They planted the Norwegian flag and carefully documented their achievement before beginning the return journey. Their success marked a major milestone in the history of exploration.

(9) More than a month later, on January 17, 1912, Scott and his companions arrived at the South Pole. There they discovered evidence that Amundsen's team had already been there. Although disappointed, Scott recorded the moment in his journal and began preparing for the difficult return trip.

(10) The journey back proved far more challenging than expected. Harsh weather, limited supplies, physical exhaustion, and extreme cold slowed the British team's progress. Despite their determination, conditions continued to worsen.

(11) Scott and his companions documented their experiences through journals and notes. These records reveal both the physical hardships they faced and their commitment to completing the expedition. Even under increasingly dangerous circumstances, the team continued carrying scientific samples collected during the journey.

(12) Tragically, Scott and the four men traveling with him did not survive the return trip. Months later, a search party discovered their final campsite along with journals and scientific materials that provided valuable information about the expedition.

(13) News of the race attracted international attention. Amundsen was celebrated as the first person to reach the South Pole, while Scott's expedition became known for its perseverance and dedication in the face of extraordinary adversity. Historians have studied both expeditions for more than a century, analyzing the decisions, preparations, and circumstances that influenced their outcomes.

(14) The Race to the South Pole remains one of the most significant events in the history of exploration. It demonstrated the importance of planning, adaptability, and leadership when confronting difficult environments. The expeditions also expanded scientific knowledge about Antarctica and inspired future generations of explorers and researchers.

(15) Today, Antarctica continues to attract scientists from around the world. Modern technology has made travel and research safer than it was during the early twentieth century, but the continent remains one of the most challenging environments on Earth. The achievements of Amundsen, Scott, and their teams remain important chapters in the history of human exploration.

questions.text:
How do the harsh conditions of Antarctica influence the events of the passage?
A. They encourage both teams to abandon their expeditions before reaching the South Pole.
B. They create challenges that affect the teams' progress, decisions, and ultimate outcomes.
C. They help both teams travel more quickly than expected.
D. They prevent the explorers from conducting any scientific research.
Answer: B

Which two details best support the idea that Amundsen's expedition emphasized preparation and efficiency?
A. Amundsen carefully studied survival techniques used by Indigenous peoples living in Arctic environments. (paragraph 3)
B. Scott's team collected geological samples and conducted research throughout the journey. (paragraph 4)
C. Amundsen's team relied heavily on skis and dog sleds and established supply depots along the route. (paragraphs 3 and 6)
D. Scott recorded the discovery of Amundsen's flag in his journal. (paragraph 9)
Answers: A and C

Complete the summary.
The passage describes the Race to the South Pole between Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott. Although both teams faced extreme Antarctic conditions, Amundsen's expedition reached the South Pole first through careful preparation and efficient travel methods. Scott's team arrived later and faced tragic difficulties during the return journey. Overall, the passage emphasizes the importance of (planning and adaptability / speed without preparation / competition over cooperation) when facing difficult challenges.
Answer: planning and adaptability

How does the author's point of view contribute to the passage?
A. The author presents only Amundsen's achievements and ignores Scott's expedition.
B. The author uses an objective perspective to explain the accomplishments, challenges, and significance of both expeditions.
C. The author argues that Scott's expedition was more important than Amundsen's.
D. The author tells the story from Scott's first-person point of view.
Answer: B
