passages.title: Saturday Morning at Delgado's Market
passages.text:
1 Every Saturday morning, Delgado's Market seemed to wake before the rest of the town. Long before most storefronts unlocked their doors, delivery trucks lined the curb outside the market. Workers unloaded crates of produce while the sky was still painted in shades of pale blue and pink. The sounds of rolling carts, opening boxes, and brief conversations echoed through the quiet streets. By the time the church bells rang seven times, the market was already alive with activity.

2 The building itself was not especially large, but it felt bigger than it appeared. Large windows stretched across the front, reflecting the growing sunlight. Striped awnings shaded the sidewalk, and handwritten signs advertised seasonal fruits, fresh bread, and local honey. People approaching the market often slowed their pace before entering, as if crossing an invisible line between the ordinary routines of the week and the unique rhythm of Saturday morning.

3 Near the entrance stood displays overflowing with color. Bright oranges were stacked in careful pyramids. Apples filled wooden crates arranged by variety and shade. Baskets held peaches, plums, and pears, each labeled with the name of the nearby farm where it had been grown. Water droplets glistened on leafy vegetables that had arrived only hours earlier. The displays looked less like grocery products and more like carefully arranged collections in a museum.

4 Inside, the market offered even more to observe. Shelves stretched from floor to ceiling, holding everything from jars of homemade jam to bags of coffee beans imported from distant countries. The air carried a mixture of scents that constantly shifted as customers moved through the aisles. One moment smelled of cinnamon and vanilla from the bakery. The next brought hints of citrus, fresh herbs, or roasted coffee.

5 At the bakery counter, a small crowd gathered throughout the morning. Behind a glass display case rested rows of pastries, muffins, and loaves of bread. Children studied the options with serious expressions while adults debated which items to bring home. Every few minutes, an employee emerged from the kitchen carrying another tray. The warm scent of butter and sugar drifted through the market, causing many shoppers to pause even when they had not planned to buy anything.

6 Conversations seemed to happen everywhere. Two neighbors discussed a community event while comparing tomatoes. A teacher greeted a former student near the dairy section. An elderly couple slowly pushed a shopping cart through the aisles, stopping frequently to speak with people they knew. Some customers arrived to purchase groceries, but many appeared equally interested in the chance to see familiar faces.

7 Near the center of the market sat a local musician with an acoustic guitar. His music blended naturally into the sounds of the building. Some songs were lively and upbeat, while others drifted quietly through the air like background conversation. Most shoppers continued with their errands, yet many unconsciously adjusted their pace to the rhythm of the music. A few dropped coins into a small case beside his chair. Others simply smiled as they passed.

8 Through the front windows, the town square remained visible. Families crossed the brick pathways carrying reusable bags. Cyclists stopped for water near a fountain. Children chased pigeons across open areas while parents watched from nearby benches. The market seemed connected to all of it. People moved in and out of the building continuously, carrying groceries, stories, and plans for the weekend.

9 By midmorning, the market reached its busiest point. Shopping carts filled the aisles, conversations overlapped, and checkout lines stretched toward nearby displays. Yet despite the crowd, the atmosphere remained calm. No one appeared rushed. Customers waited patiently, employees greeted visitors by name, and strangers occasionally offered recommendations about favorite products.

10 For many residents, Delgado's Market represented more than a place to buy food. It served as a weekly gathering place. New residents met neighbors there. Community groups posted announcements near the entrance. Local farmers shared information about upcoming harvests. The market quietly connected people who might otherwise never cross paths.

11 As noon approached, the energy gradually changed. The longest lines disappeared. The musician packed away his guitar. Vendors restocked displays and swept small bits of debris from the floor. Sunlight streamed through the windows at a different angle, casting long shadows across the aisles. The building felt calmer, though no less welcoming.

12 Traces of the morning remained everywhere. Empty pastry trays sat behind the bakery counter. Handwritten signs showed which products had already sold out. A faint scent of fresh bread lingered in the air. Outside, shoppers carried bags toward parked cars or nearby homes. The market would continue operating throughout the afternoon, but its busiest hours had passed.

13 Yet the significance of Delgado's Market extended beyond any single Saturday. Week after week, season after season, the same traditions continued. The faces changed over time, new products appeared, and old businesses came and went, but the market remained a familiar part of community life.

14 As the afternoon sun climbed higher above the square, Delgado's Market settled into a quieter rhythm. The sounds softened, the crowds thinned, and the pace slowed. Still, there was a sense that the morning's activity lingered in the building itself. The conversations, laughter, music, and routines had become part of the place, waiting to return when another Saturday morning arrived.

old and rarely touched by customers

passages.title: The Public Library After School
passages.text:
1 Every weekday afternoon, the public library transformed into a different place. During the morning hours, the building was quiet enough that visitors could hear pages turning and keyboards clicking. By three o'clock, however, the atmosphere changed. Students arrived carrying backpacks, parents stopped by with younger children, and the reading rooms gradually filled with activity.

2 Sunlight streamed through tall windows overlooking the town square. Dust drifted through the beams of light, moving slowly above rows of bookshelves. The shelves themselves seemed endless, stretching across rooms filled with novels, biographies, reference materials, and magazines. Some books looked brand new, while others showed signs of being borrowed hundreds of times.

3 Near the entrance, a bulletin board displayed announcements for community events, sports leagues, volunteer opportunities, and local performances. Visitors often paused to read the notices before continuing deeper into the building. Some left carrying books. Others left carrying information about opportunities they had not known existed.

4 The teen section became one of the busiest areas in the library after school. Students gathered around tables to complete assignments, compare notes, or work on group projects. Laptops opened, notebooks spread across tabletops, and quiet conversations filled the space. Although signs requested silence, the low hum of discussion had become part of the library's daily rhythm.

5 Across the room, several students sat alone wearing headphones while studying. Some highlighted passages in textbooks. Others reviewed flashcards or typed essays. The library seemed to accommodate every learning style at once. Those seeking collaboration found it. Those preferring solitude found that as well.

6 The children's section offered a different atmosphere. Brightly colored rugs covered the floor, and picture books filled low shelves designed for younger readers. Parents sat beside children while reading stories aloud. Occasionally, laughter drifted across the room when someone discovered a favorite character or a particularly funny illustration.

7 Behind a long desk near the center of the building, librarians answered questions throughout the afternoon. Some visitors needed help locating specific books. Others asked for recommendations. A few sought assistance with research projects or technology issues. The librarians moved from one request to another with practiced patience.

8 As evening approached, the crowd gradually changed. Students packed away assignments and headed home. Adults arrived after work to browse shelves or attend community programs. The sunlight faded beyond the windows, and lamps cast a warm glow across the reading areas.

9 By closing time, the library had grown quiet once more. Chairs sat neatly arranged around tables, books waited on shelves, and computer screens darkened. Yet traces of the afternoon remained. Half-finished notes, checked-out books, and newly discovered interests had all begun their journeys within those walls.

passages.title: The Community Skate Park at Sunset
passages.text:
1 Every evening, as the sun began its slow descent behind the rooftops, the community skate park came alive. During the hottest hours of the day, only a few people used the ramps and rails. As temperatures cooled, however, skateboarders, scooter riders, and spectators gradually filled the concrete space.

2 The park occupied a corner of a larger recreation area. Smooth ramps curved upward beside stair sets, rails, and bowls designed for riders of varying skill levels. The surfaces showed signs of constant use. Scuff marks covered the concrete, and scratches decorated the rails. To many visitors, those marks looked like evidence of thousands of attempts, successes, and failures.

3 The sounds of the skate park formed a unique soundtrack. Wheels rattled across concrete. Skateboards struck the ground after jumps. Riders called encouragement to one another from opposite ends of the park. Occasionally, cheers erupted when someone successfully landed a difficult trick after numerous attempts.

4 Experienced riders often occupied the larger ramps. They moved with confidence, linking tricks together in smooth sequences. Younger riders usually gathered nearby, watching carefully and occasionally attempting simplified versions of what they observed.

5 Unlike organized sports, the skate park had no coaches directing activities. Instead, participants learned from observation, practice, and one another. Advice flowed freely between strangers. An experienced rider might spend several minutes helping a beginner understand a new technique before returning to practice.

6 Benches surrounding the park filled with spectators as the evening progressed. Parents watched younger children. Friends recorded videos on their phones. Some visitors never entered the riding area at all. They simply enjoyed observing the energy and movement around them.

7 The setting sun gradually changed the appearance of the park. Long shadows stretched across the ramps. Golden light reflected from metal rails. For a brief period each evening, the entire space seemed to glow.

8 Even mistakes became part of the experience. Riders occasionally lost balance, missed landings, or abandoned difficult attempts. Most responded by getting up and trying again. Failure appeared less like an ending and more like a step in the learning process.

9 As darkness approached, riders began gathering their equipment. Conversations continued as people prepared to leave. The ramps emptied, the noise faded, and the park returned to stillness. Yet the determination, creativity, and community visible each evening seemed to linger long after the last skateboard rolled away.

passages.title: The Lost Notebook
passages.text:
1 Ava noticed the notebook at the end of the school day. It sat alone on a bench outside the science building while students hurried toward buses, sports practices, and after-school activities. At first, she walked past it. Then she stopped and looked back. The notebook appeared new, with a blue cover and a school identification sticker attached to the front.

2 Curious, Ava picked it up. The sticker identified the owner as Ethan Morales, an eighth-grade student she recognized but did not know well. She considered turning the notebook in to the office the next morning. However, as she slipped it into her backpack, she remembered hearing several teachers discuss a major science project due the following week.

3 That evening, Ava opened the notebook to look for contact information. Instead, she discovered page after page of notes, sketches, diagrams, and research related to the project. Detailed observations filled nearly every page. Some entries included corrections and revisions, suggesting that Ethan had spent weeks preparing the assignment.

4 Ava closed the notebook immediately. She knew she should not read any further. Still, she felt concerned. Losing the notebook so close to the deadline could create serious problems for its owner.

5 The next morning, Ava arrived at school early and brought the notebook to the main office. Unfortunately, the secretary explained that Ethan had already left for a field trip with the science club and would not return until the following day.

6 As Ava walked toward her first class, she spotted a group of students discussing the project.

7 "Did you hear about Ethan?" one student asked.

8 "Yeah," another replied. "Apparently, he lost all of his research."

9 Ava paused. She could easily solve the problem by leaving the notebook at the office. Eventually, Ethan would find it. Yet she wondered whether there was a faster way.

10 During lunch, she spoke with a science teacher who supervised the club. The teacher agreed to contact the group and arrange for the notebook to be delivered to the field trip site.

11 The following afternoon, Ethan approached Ava in the hallway.

12 "Thank you," he said. "I thought I was going to have to start over."

13 Ava smiled. "I figured you'd probably want it back."

14 Ethan laughed. "Probably."

15 For the rest of the day, Ava felt unexpectedly happy. Returning the notebook had not taken much effort, yet she realized that a small decision had made a significant difference for someone else.

passages.title: The Empty Chair
passages.text:
1 Every Wednesday afternoon, the members of the school chess club gathered in Room 214. Some students joined because they enjoyed competition. Others appreciated the opportunity to spend time with friends. Regardless of their reasons, everyone looked forward to the weekly meetings.

2 For nearly two years, one chair near the front of the room had belonged to Noah. He was not the strongest player in the club, but he rarely missed a meeting. He greeted new members, organized tournaments, and encouraged younger students who were still learning the game.

3 One week, Noah's chair remained empty.

4 At first, nobody thought much about it. Students occasionally missed meetings because of appointments, sports practices, or family obligations.

5 The following week, however, the chair remained empty again.

6 Eventually, the club advisor explained that Noah had moved to another state after a family member accepted a new job. The move happened quickly, leaving little time for goodbyes.

7 The room felt different after the announcement. Conversations continued, and games were played as usual, but many students noticed the absence. Small routines Noah had established suddenly disappeared.

8 During the next meeting, club members discussed ways to stay in touch. Several students suggested creating a group message. Others proposed organizing an online chess match.

9 "We should do something more permanent," said Lily, one of the club officers.

10 The group agreed.

11 Over the next several weeks, students worked together on a project. They collected photographs from tournaments, gathered written memories, and asked club members to contribute notes describing their favorite experiences with Noah.

12 Some stories were funny. Others focused on moments when Noah helped new players feel welcome. As the collection grew, students realized how many people he had influenced during his time in the club.

13 At the final meeting of the semester, the group presented the completed project during a video call with Noah. The collection included messages from current members, former members, teachers, and parents.

14 Noah appeared surprised as he scrolled through the pages.

15 "I didn't realize people remembered all of this," he said.

16 "We do," Lily replied.

17 The room filled with smiles.

18 Long after the call ended, the students continued talking about the project. Although Noah was no longer sitting in the chair near the front of the room, his presence still felt like part of the club. The project had reminded everyone that the impact a person has on others often lasts longer than the time they spend together.

Short Stories: complete narratives in brief format

B  In "The Lost Notebook," the response unfolds over a few days, while in "The Empty Chair," the response builds across multiple weekly meetings.
C  Both passages open by stating the central problem in the first paragraph and then work backward to explain its cause.
D  Both passages end before the affected character ever learns what was done on his behalf.

title: From Videos to Ventures: The Story of MrBeast

passages.title: From Videos to Ventures: The Story of MrBeast
passages.text:
1 Millions of people around the world know Jimmy Donaldson by a different name: MrBeast. His videos regularly attract audiences larger than the populations of many countries, and his challenges, giveaways, and large-scale projects have made him one of the most recognizable creators on the internet. While many people see only the finished videos, Donaldson's success resulted from years of experimentation, persistence, and a willingness to learn from failure.

2 Donaldson was born on May 7, 1998, and grew up in North Carolina. Like many teenagers, he spent time watching videos online and exploring different interests. However, he became especially fascinated by YouTube. Rather than viewing the platform only as entertainment, he became curious about how videos attracted viewers and why some creators achieved enormous success while others struggled to build an audience.

3 At the age of thirteen, Donaldson began uploading videos to YouTube. His earliest content looked very different from the videos that later made him famous. He experimented with gaming videos, commentary, reactions, and a variety of other formats. Most of these videos attracted only a small number of viewers. While some young creators might have become discouraged, Donaldson remained focused on improving.

4 Over the next several years, he devoted countless hours to studying the platform. He analyzed successful channels, discussed ideas with other creators, and paid close attention to viewer behavior. He often experimented with different titles, thumbnails, and video concepts to determine what captured people's attention. During this period, his channel grew slowly, but he viewed every project as an opportunity to learn.

5 Eventually, Donaldson began creating unusual challenge videos that stood out from much of the content on YouTube. One early video featured him counting to 100,000 on camera, a process that took dozens of hours. Another involved watching a music video repeatedly for an extended period. These ideas were unusual, but they demonstrated his willingness to try concepts that others might overlook.

6 As his audience expanded, Donaldson continued increasing the scale of his projects. Rather than repeating the same formula, he searched for new ways to surprise viewers. Some videos featured elaborate competitions with large cash prizes. Others involved building enormous sets, organizing challenges, or recreating activities inspired by television programs and games.

7 Success brought new opportunities, but it also created new challenges. Producing large-scale videos required careful planning, significant resources, and a growing team of employees. Every project involved risks. If a video failed to attract viewers, the time and money invested in its production could be difficult to recover. Nevertheless, Donaldson continued experimenting because he believed innovation was essential to maintaining audience interest.

8 One aspect of MrBeast's content that attracted particular attention involved philanthropy. Over time, many of his videos focused on helping individuals, communities, and charitable causes. Some projects involved donating money to people facing financial hardships. Others focused on larger efforts such as supporting food banks, funding environmental initiatives, or assisting communities with access to clean water.

9 These projects often generated public discussion. Supporters argued that the videos used entertainment to draw attention to important issues while helping people in meaningful ways. Others debated whether charitable efforts should be featured in online content. Regardless of differing opinions, the projects increased public awareness of various causes and demonstrated the potential influence of digital media.

10 Beyond YouTube, Donaldson expanded into business ventures. He launched companies, developed products, and explored new ways to connect with audiences. These efforts required many of the same skills that helped him succeed as a creator, including creativity, planning, adaptability, and an understanding of what interested consumers.

11 Despite his success, Donaldson has often spoken about the amount of work required to achieve his goals. Interviews frequently highlight his intense focus on improvement and his willingness to spend years refining his craft. He has explained that many successful projects were built upon lessons learned from earlier mistakes and unsuccessful experiments.

12 His story illustrates an important reality about achievement. While talent can provide advantages, long-term success often depends upon persistence and a willingness to continue learning. Donaldson did not become one of the world's most successful content creators overnight. Instead, his accomplishments developed through years of effort, experimentation, and gradual improvement.

13 Today, MrBeast remains one of the most influential figures in digital media. His videos continue attracting massive audiences, and his projects regularly generate conversations about creativity, entrepreneurship, and philanthropy. Whether people admire his business strategies, his charitable efforts, or his ability to entertain millions of viewers, his journey demonstrates how curiosity and determination can transform a simple interest into a global career.

D  "Donaldson was born on May 7, 1998, and grew up in North Carolina." (paragraph 2)
E  "Some videos featured elaborate competitions with large cash prizes." (paragraph 6)

promoting one's own products for profit

planning videos that surprise viewers

passages.title: Coco Gauff: Growing Into Greatness
passages.text:
1 Coco Gauff became famous before most teenagers had even finished high school. At fifteen years old, she stepped onto the grass courts of Wimbledon and defeated Venus Williams, one of the players she had admired since childhood. The victory shocked many tennis fans and introduced Gauff to the world. Yet her story did not end with one surprising match. In the years that followed, she had to prove that early attention could become lasting success.

2 Gauff was born on March 13, 2004, in Florida. Her parents had both been college athletes, so sports were part of her life from an early age. She began playing tennis as a child and quickly showed unusual talent. Still, talent alone was not enough. Gauff and her family made serious decisions about training, travel, and education so she could pursue the sport at a high level.

3 As Gauff improved, she entered more competitive tournaments and began facing older, more experienced players. Her speed, focus, and powerful strokes helped her stand out. Coaches and tennis fans noticed not only her athletic ability but also her confidence. Even as a young player, Gauff carried herself like someone who believed she belonged on the biggest stages.

4 In 2019, Gauff received the opportunity that changed her career. At Wimbledon, one of tennis's most important tournaments, she qualified for the main draw at age fifteen. Her first-round opponent was Venus Williams, a former Wimbledon champion. For many young players, facing a legend would have been overwhelming. Gauff, however, stayed composed and won the match.

5 The victory brought instant attention. News outlets told her story, fans followed her matches, and people began calling her a future star. That kind of attention can be exciting, but it can also create pressure. Suddenly, every match seemed important. Every win was celebrated, and every loss was analyzed. Gauff had to learn how to compete while millions of people watched her progress.

6 Over the next few years, Gauff continued developing as a player. She won matches, lost difficult ones, changed parts of her game, and gained experience on different surfaces. Like many athletes, she faced setbacks. Some tournaments ended earlier than she hoped. Some opponents exposed weaknesses she needed to improve. Instead of disappearing after her first moment of fame, Gauff kept working.

7 One important part of Gauff's growth was learning how to handle expectations. Because she had succeeded so young, many people expected her to win major titles quickly. However, professional tennis is demanding. Players must manage travel, training, injuries, pressure, and constant competition. Gauff's progress showed that becoming great often requires patience as well as talent.

8 In 2023, Gauff achieved one of her biggest goals when she won the U.S. Open. The tournament took place in New York, in front of loud crowds and intense media attention. By winning the title, Gauff proved that she could succeed not only as a promising young player but as a champion. The victory became a defining moment in her career.

9 Gauff's success continued beyond that single tournament. She became one of the top players in the world and kept competing against the strongest athletes in the sport. In 2025, she won the French Open, adding another major title to her career. Winning on different stages showed that her success was not the result of one lucky run. It was the result of continued growth.

10 Gauff has also become known for her maturity. In interviews, she often speaks thoughtfully about pressure, improvement, and learning from mistakes. Many athletes prefer to focus only on winning, but Gauff has openly acknowledged that losses can teach important lessons. Her ability to reflect on both success and disappointment has helped her continue moving forward.

11 Another reason Gauff appeals to many young fans is that she seems connected to their generation. She has spoken about enjoying music, fashion, and popular culture, and she has grown up during the same digital age as many of her supporters. While she is an elite athlete, she also feels familiar to students who see parts of their own interests reflected in her personality.

12 Still, Gauff's influence reaches beyond popularity. She represents what can happen when early talent is matched with discipline and resilience. Her career shows that being noticed is not the same as being finished. Fame may open a door, but effort determines what happens afterward.

13 Today, Coco Gauff remains one of the most exciting athletes in tennis. Her journey from a fifteen-year-old defeating a legend to a major champion offers more than a sports story. It shows the value of confidence, patience, and continued improvement. Gauff's achievements remind people that greatness is not built in one moment. It grows over time.

passages.title: Mark Rober: Engineering Curiosity
passages.text:
1 Millions of people know Mark Rober as a creator of entertaining science and engineering videos. Through his YouTube channel, he has designed everything from elaborate obstacle courses for squirrels to devices that discourage package theft. While his projects often appear playful, they are rooted in scientific thinking, problem-solving, and creativity. Rober's career demonstrates how curiosity can lead to unexpected opportunities.

2 Rober grew up with a strong interest in understanding how things worked. As a child, he enjoyed building, experimenting, and finding creative solutions to problems. Instead of simply accepting how objects functioned, he often asked questions and looked for ways to improve them. This habit of exploring ideas would later become an important part of his success.

3 After completing his education, Rober pursued a career in engineering. He eventually worked for NASA, where he spent several years contributing to projects related to space exploration. One of the most notable projects during his time at NASA involved the Curiosity rover, a robotic vehicle designed to explore Mars. Engineers working on the mission faced countless technical challenges, and every decision required careful planning and testing.

4 Working on space missions taught Rober the importance of persistence. Complex engineering projects rarely succeed on the first attempt. Designs must be tested, modified, and tested again. Problems that appear impossible often require teams to experiment with multiple solutions before finding one that works. These experiences helped shape the way Rober approached future projects.

5 Although he enjoyed engineering, Rober also wanted to share his enthusiasm for science with a wider audience. He began creating videos that explained scientific concepts through demonstrations and challenges. Rather than presenting information like a traditional lecture, he focused on making learning entertaining and accessible.

6 One of Rober's strengths is his ability to transform everyday questions into engaging investigations. A simple curiosity about animal behavior, physics, or engineering can become a large-scale experiment involving careful planning, data collection, and analysis. His videos often show both successes and failures, helping viewers understand that problem-solving is a process rather than a single moment of discovery.

7 Over time, Rober's audience grew dramatically. Millions of students, teachers, and families began watching his videos. Many educators appreciated the way he encouraged scientific thinking. Instead of simply providing answers, he demonstrated how questions can lead to experimentation and learning.

8 Rober has also used his platform to support larger educational initiatives. He has collaborated with scientists, engineers, and organizations to promote science education and inspire young people to explore careers in STEM fields. Through these efforts, he has reached audiences far beyond traditional classrooms.

9 Today, Mark Rober continues creating projects that combine engineering, creativity, and entertainment. His work shows that learning does not have to be separate from fun. By encouraging curiosity and experimentation, he has inspired countless people to view science not as a collection of facts but as a way of exploring the world.

passages.title: Boyan Slat: Engineering for Change
passages.text:
1 While many teenagers spend their free time pursuing hobbies or sports, Boyan Slat became focused on a global environmental problem. During a diving trip as a teenager, he noticed large amounts of plastic waste floating in the ocean. The experience left a lasting impression and sparked a question that would shape the course of his life: Could technology help remove plastic pollution from the world's oceans?

2 Slat grew up in the Netherlands and developed an interest in science and engineering at an early age. He enjoyed designing projects and thinking about how technology could solve complex problems. After observing the extent of ocean pollution firsthand, he became determined to explore possible solutions.

3 Many people assumed that cleaning large areas of the ocean would be impossible. The amount of plastic waste was enormous, and traditional cleanup methods seemed inefficient. Rather than accepting these limitations, Slat began researching alternative approaches. He studied ocean currents, engineering principles, and environmental science to better understand the challenge.

4 As a teenager, Slat presented an idea for a system that could use the movement of ocean currents to help collect floating plastic. The concept attracted attention because it approached the problem differently from existing methods. Instead of chasing plastic through the water, the system would allow natural forces to help bring debris toward collection devices.

5 Turning an idea into reality proved far more difficult than developing the original concept. Slat and his team faced engineering obstacles, financial challenges, and public skepticism. Many experts questioned whether the technology could succeed on a meaningful scale. Some believed the project was too ambitious.

6 Despite these challenges, Slat remained committed to improving the design. His organization, The Ocean Cleanup, conducted tests, gathered data, and refined its systems over several years. Some early attempts produced disappointing results, but each setback provided information that helped guide future improvements.

7 As the technology advanced, cleanup systems began removing increasing amounts of plastic from the ocean. The organization also expanded its efforts to rivers, recognizing that preventing plastic from reaching the ocean could be just as important as removing pollution that was already there.

8 Beyond the engineering achievements, Slat's work demonstrated the value of persistence. Many large-scale problems cannot be solved quickly. They require years of research, collaboration, and adaptation. Slat's willingness to continue working toward a difficult goal became an important part of his story.

9 Today, Boyan Slat is recognized internationally for his environmental efforts. While ocean pollution remains a significant challenge, his work has shown that innovative thinking can contribute to meaningful progress. His story encourages young people to believe that even complex global problems can be addressed through creativity, determination, and scientific problem-solving.

passages.title: Simone Biles: Redefining Success
passages.text:
1 Simone Biles is widely considered one of the greatest gymnasts in history. Her achievements have transformed the sport, inspired millions of fans, and demonstrated that success involves more than winning medals. Through extraordinary athletic performances and difficult personal decisions, Biles has shown that excellence requires both physical skill and mental strength.

2 Biles was born in Columbus, Ohio, in 1997. As a young child, she displayed unusual athletic ability and energy. When she visited a gymnastics facility during a field trip, coaches immediately noticed her natural talent. Soon afterward, she began training seriously and quickly progressed through increasingly competitive levels of the sport.

3 Gymnastics is one of the most demanding athletic disciplines in the world. Athletes spend countless hours practicing skills that require strength, balance, flexibility, and precision. Even small mistakes can lead to injuries or lower scores. Biles embraced these challenges and developed a reputation for exceptional work ethic.

4 As her skills improved, Biles began competing nationally and internationally. Judges, coaches, and fellow athletes recognized that she was performing routines unlike anything they had seen before. Some of her skills were so difficult that other gymnasts rarely attempted them in competition. Eventually, several moves were officially named after her because she was the first woman to perform them successfully at major events.

5 Biles earned worldwide attention during the 2016 Olympic Games in Rio de Janeiro. There, she won multiple gold medals and established herself as one of the most dominant athletes in the competition. Fans admired not only her success but also the confidence and joy she displayed while competing.

6 Following the Olympics, expectations grew even higher. Many athletes struggle when faced with constant public attention, but Biles continued to train and compete at an elite level. She added more championships and records to her career while helping expand interest in gymnastics around the world.

7 One of the most significant moments in Biles's career occurred during the 2021 Olympic Games in Tokyo. During the competition, she experienced difficulties that affected her ability to safely perform certain skills. Recognizing the risks involved, she made the difficult decision to withdraw from several events.

8 The decision sparked widespread discussion. Some people questioned whether athletes should continue competing despite challenges. Others praised Biles for prioritizing her health and safety. Regardless of differing opinions, her choice highlighted the importance of mental well-being in high-performance environments.

9 In the years that followed, Biles returned to competition and continued achieving remarkable results. Her comeback demonstrated resilience and determination. Rather than allowing setbacks or criticism to define her career, she continued moving forward.

10 Today, Simone Biles is admired not only for her medals and records but also for her willingness to redefine what strength looks like. Her story reminds people that true success involves making difficult decisions, learning from challenges, and remaining committed to personal growth.

passages.title: Shohei Ohtani: Changing the Game
passages.text:
1 Throughout the history of professional baseball, players have generally specialized in one role. Some focus on pitching, while others concentrate on hitting. Shohei Ohtani challenged that tradition by excelling at both. His unique combination of skills has led many experts to describe him as one of the most extraordinary athletes in modern sports.

2 Ohtani was born in Japan in 1994 and developed a passion for baseball at a young age. From the beginning, he showed unusual ability both as a pitcher and as a hitter. While many coaches encouraged athletes to specialize in one area, Ohtani continued developing both aspects of his game.

3 As he advanced through youth and professional baseball, Ohtani's talent became increasingly apparent. His fastballs reached impressive speeds, and he demonstrated the ability to strike out opposing hitters. At the same time, he displayed remarkable power at the plate, hitting home runs that attracted attention throughout Japan.

4 Professional baseball organizations often prefer players to focus on a single position because specialization can improve performance. However, Ohtani believed he could contribute in multiple ways. His willingness to pursue an unconventional path required confidence and persistence.

5 After establishing himself as a star in Japan, Ohtani moved to Major League Baseball in the United States. The transition presented new challenges. He faced stronger competition, increased media attention, and the pressure of proving that his unique approach could succeed at the highest level.

6 Injuries created additional obstacles. At various points in his career, Ohtani required rehabilitation and recovery time. Some observers wondered whether it would be possible for him to continue performing both roles effectively.

7 Rather than abandoning his goals, Ohtani continued refining his skills. Through careful training and preparation, he demonstrated that an athlete could excel as both a pitcher and a hitter. His performances challenged long-standing assumptions about what baseball players could accomplish.

8 Ohtani's success attracted fans from around the world. Many people were fascinated by his versatility and willingness to attempt something that few athletes had pursued. His achievements helped introduce new audiences to baseball and generated excitement throughout the sport.

9 Beyond statistics and awards, Ohtani's career illustrates the value of innovation. Instead of following the traditional path, he explored a different possibility and worked tirelessly to make it successful. His example encourages people to question assumptions and consider new approaches to familiar challenges.

10 Today, Shohei Ohtani remains one of the most recognizable figures in professional sports. His story demonstrates that progress often begins when someone is willing to pursue an idea that others believe is impossible.

title: The Day the Mountain Woke: Mount St. Helens Erupts

passages.title: The Day the Mountain Woke: Mount St. Helens Erupts
passages.text:
1 On the morning of May 18, 1980, many people near Mount St. Helens expected an ordinary Sunday. The mountain stood in southwestern Washington State, its snow-covered peak rising above forests, rivers, and small communities. For generations, it had been one of the most recognizable landmarks in the region. Although scientists had been closely monitoring the volcano for weeks, few people could fully imagine what was about to happen.

2 The months leading up to that morning had been unusual. In March 1980, a series of earthquakes began occurring beneath the mountain. Residents felt tremors shake homes and rattle windows. Soon afterward, steam explosions blasted ash high into the sky. Scientists recognized these events as signs that the volcano was becoming active. As a result, government officials established restricted areas around the mountain to protect public safety.

3 Despite the warnings, many people remained fascinated by the volcano. News crews arrived to document the activity, photographers searched for dramatic images, and curious visitors traveled to nearby locations hoping to witness history. Local business owners sold souvenirs, and conversations throughout the region focused on one question: Would the mountain erupt?

4 One of the people closely observing the volcano was David Johnston, a volcanologist with the United States Geological Survey. Johnston and other scientists worked long hours studying changes in the mountain's shape and behavior. Instruments revealed that part of the volcano's northern side was swelling outward. The bulge grew larger each day, suggesting that pressure was building beneath the surface.

5 At 8:32 on the morning of May 18, a powerful earthquake struck beneath the mountain. Within seconds, the northern side of Mount St. Helens collapsed in the largest landslide ever recorded in modern history. Millions of tons of rock and debris rushed down the mountain at tremendous speed. The sudden collapse released pressure that had been trapped underground.

6 What happened next stunned scientists and observers alike. A massive lateral blast exploded from the side of the volcano. Instead of erupting straight upward like many people expected, the explosion surged sideways across the landscape. Trees snapped like matchsticks as the blast raced outward. Entire forests were flattened within moments. The force of the eruption was so powerful that it dramatically reshaped the mountain itself.

7 Ash, gas, and volcanic material shot high into the atmosphere. The eruption column eventually reached more than fifteen miles into the sky. Winds carried ash across several states, turning daytime skies dark in some communities. People hundreds of miles away watched ash settle onto cars, streets, rooftops, and fields.

8 Communication systems quickly became overwhelmed as officials worked to understand the scope of the disaster. Roads were buried, bridges were destroyed, and rivers became clogged with mud and debris. Pilots reported seeing enormous clouds of ash stretching across the horizon. Residents throughout the region listened closely to radio reports as new information emerged.

9 Scientists monitoring the eruption faced dangers of their own. David Johnston was stationed at an observation post several miles from the volcano. Moments after the eruption began, he radioed a warning to colleagues. His message helped alert others to the scale of the event. Tragically, Johnston did not survive the eruption. Today, he is remembered for his dedication to scientific research and public safety.

10 In the weeks that followed, people struggled to understand the extent of the destruction. Entire forests had disappeared. Wildlife habitats were transformed. Lakes, rivers, and valleys looked completely different from the way they had appeared before the eruption. In some areas, the landscape seemed almost unrecognizable.

11 Yet amid the devastation, scientists discovered something remarkable. Life began returning sooner than expected. Plants emerged from the ash-covered ground. Animals gradually returned to affected areas. Researchers realized they had an extraordinary opportunity to study how ecosystems recover after major natural disasters.

12 Over the following decades, Mount St. Helens became one of the most closely studied volcanoes in the world. Scientists used information gathered from the eruption to improve volcanic monitoring systems and emergency planning. The lessons learned helped communities better prepare for future volcanic events both in the United States and around the world.

13 Today, visitors to Mount St. Helens can still see evidence of the eruption. Portions of the mountain remain dramatically altered, serving as reminders of the immense power of natural forces. At the same time, forests, plants, and wildlife continue to grow and adapt throughout the region.

14 The eruption of Mount St. Helens remains one of the most significant natural events in modern American history. It demonstrated both the destructive power of volcanoes and the resilience of nature. More than four decades later, the story continues to fascinate scientists, students, and visitors who want to understand what happened on the day the mountain woke.

title: Alone in the Pacific: The Journey of Jessica Watson

passages.title: Alone in the Pacific: The Journey of Jessica Watson
passages.text:
1 On October 18, 2009, sixteen-year-old Jessica Watson guided her bright pink sailboat out of Sydney Harbour in Australia and into the open ocean. Thousands of people watched from the shoreline as she began a journey that many experienced sailors considered impossible. Her goal was to sail around the world alone, without stopping and without assistance. If she succeeded, she would become one of the youngest people ever to complete such a voyage.

2 Watson had not made the decision suddenly. She had spent years learning how to sail and preparing for the challenges of ocean travel. Sailing a boat around the world required far more than simply steering in the right direction. A sailor needed to understand weather systems, navigation, boat maintenance, emergency procedures, and survival skills. Even small mistakes could become dangerous when someone was alone in the middle of the ocean.

3 Before her departure, many people questioned whether the voyage was a good idea. Some critics argued that she was too young to face the risks involved. Others worried about storms, equipment failures, and the isolation she would experience during months at sea. Supporters admired her determination and preparation, but even they understood that the journey would be difficult.

4 Watson listened carefully to the concerns, but she remained committed to her goal. She believed that challenging adventures required careful planning rather than fear. Instead of focusing on doubts, she concentrated on preparing herself and her boat. Months of training, practice voyages, and safety drills helped build her confidence before she ever left shore.

5 Once Watson entered the Pacific Ocean, the reality of the challenge quickly became clear. She spent long days completely alone. There were no teammates to assist her and no family members nearby to offer encouragement. Much of her time involved monitoring weather reports, adjusting sails, checking equipment, and making navigation decisions.

6 The ocean presented constant obstacles. Powerful waves rocked the boat. Strong winds demanded careful sail adjustments. At times, Watson encountered rough weather that made even simple tasks difficult. Cooking, sleeping, and moving around the cabin became challenging when the boat pitched and rolled through large swells.

7 One of the most frightening moments occurred before the official voyage had even begun. During a training trip, Watson's boat collided with a large cargo ship at night. The crash damaged the vessel and could have convinced her to abandon her plans. Instead, repairs were completed, lessons were learned, and she continued preparing for the journey ahead.

8 As the months passed, Watson crossed vast stretches of ocean. She sailed through regions known for unpredictable weather and enormous waves. Some storms forced her to remain alert for hours at a time. During these periods, sleep became scarce as she focused on keeping the boat safe and on course.

9 Isolation created challenges of its own. Unlike many adventures that involve teams, Watson spent most of the voyage with no one physically nearby. She communicated occasionally through technology, but much of her experience involved managing responsibilities independently. She celebrated successes alone and solved problems alone.

10 Despite these difficulties, Watson found moments of wonder throughout the journey. She described seeing brilliant sunsets, clear night skies filled with stars, and marine life swimming near the boat. Dolphins occasionally appeared alongside her vessel, and the open ocean provided views that few people ever experience.

11 The voyage also required patience. Progress across the ocean was often slower than expected. Weather conditions sometimes forced Watson to change course or reduce speed. Rather than becoming frustrated, she learned to adapt. Successful sailors understand that they cannot control the wind or waves. They can only respond effectively to changing conditions.

12 After more than two hundred days at sea, Watson finally approached Australia once again. News of her progress had attracted international attention. Supporters followed her journey through updates and reports, eagerly awaiting her return.

13 On May 15, 2010, Watson sailed into Sydney Harbour, completing her voyage after more than seven months alone at sea. Thousands of people gathered to welcome her home. The teenager who had departed months earlier had successfully circled the globe.

14 Watson's accomplishment sparked widespread discussion. Some people focused on the risks she had faced. Others emphasized the preparation, determination, and skill required to complete such a demanding challenge. Regardless of their opinions, few questioned the effort involved in achieving her goal.

15 Today, Jessica Watson's voyage remains one of the most remarkable sailing adventures ever completed by a young person. Her story is not simply about traveling around the world. It is about setting an ambitious goal, preparing thoroughly, and persevering through challenges that might cause others to quit. More than a decade later, her journey continues to inspire people who dream of accomplishing something difficult.

title: Trapped Underground: The Thai Cave Rescue

passages.title: Trapped Underground: The Thai Cave Rescue
passages.text:
1 On June 23, 2018, twelve members of a youth soccer team and their coach entered the Tham Luang cave system in northern Thailand. The outing was meant to be a brief adventure after practice. The group planned to explore the caves for a short time before returning home. However, while they were underground, heavy rains began falling in the surrounding mountains. Water quickly flooded portions of the cave, blocking the route they had used to enter.

2 When the team failed to return, families became concerned. Soon, local authorities began searching for the missing group. Bicycles and personal belongings found near the cave entrance confirmed that the team was likely still inside. What began as a local search effort quickly grew into an international rescue operation.

3 The challenge facing rescuers was extraordinary. The cave system stretched for miles and included narrow passages, steep chambers, and sections that had become completely flooded. Divers could not simply swim directly to the trapped group. In some places, visibility was nearly zero because muddy water filled the tunnels. Progress was slow and dangerous.

4 As days passed, rescue teams from around the world arrived to help. Experienced cave divers, military personnel, engineers, medical experts, and volunteers worked together. Pumps were installed to remove water from the cave. Supplies were delivered whenever possible. Despite these efforts, no one knew exactly where the team was located.

5 More than a week after the group disappeared, two British cave divers made a remarkable discovery. Deep inside the cave, they found all thirteen members of the team alive. The boys and their coach were stranded on a small area of elevated ground surrounded by water. Although they were weak and hungry, they had survived by conserving energy and remaining calm.

6 Finding the team solved one problem but created another. Rescuers now faced the difficult task of bringing them out safely. The route back to the cave entrance required navigating flooded passages that challenged even expert divers. Most of the boys had little or no experience swimming in such conditions.

7 Rescue planners carefully considered multiple options. Some experts suggested waiting until floodwaters receded, but weather forecasts predicted additional rain. Others proposed teaching the boys advanced diving techniques, though time was limited. Every possible solution involved significant risk.

8 Eventually, officials approved a carefully coordinated rescue plan. Each boy would be accompanied by highly trained divers who would guide them through the flooded sections of the cave. The operation required detailed planning, specialized equipment, and cooperation among hundreds of people.

9 Over several days, rescuers successfully brought all twelve boys and their coach out of the cave. The achievement was celebrated around the world. Many people viewed the rescue as an example of courage, teamwork, and international cooperation. What had begun as a frightening situation ended with a remarkable success.

10 Today, the Thai cave rescue remains one of the most extraordinary rescue operations in modern history. It demonstrated how preparation, expertise, and collaboration can help people overcome challenges that initially seem impossible.

passages.title: The Endurance Expedition
passages.text:
1 In 1914, explorer Ernest Shackleton set out on an ambitious mission. His goal was to lead the first expedition to cross Antarctica by land. At the time, Antarctica remained one of the least explored places on Earth. Vast stretches of ice, extreme weather, and remote conditions made exploration both difficult and dangerous.

2 Shackleton and his crew sailed aboard a ship called the Endurance. After leaving South America, they headed toward Antarctica. The expedition carried supplies, equipment, and high hopes for success. However, nature had different plans.

3 Before the crew could begin the planned crossing, sea ice trapped the Endurance. At first, the explorers hoped changing conditions would free the ship. Instead, the ice tightened its grip. For months, the vessel drifted while frozen in place.

4 Eventually, the pressure became too great. Massive sheets of ice crushed the ship's wooden structure. The crew watched as their vessel was damaged beyond repair. Realizing that the Endurance could not be saved, Shackleton ordered the men to abandon ship.

5 The situation was serious. The explorers were stranded on drifting ice hundreds of miles from safety. Temperatures remained extremely cold, and supplies were limited. Yet Shackleton focused on maintaining morale. He encouraged routines, assigned responsibilities, and worked to keep the crew united.

6 Months passed as the men camped on floating ice. They endured freezing winds, uncertainty, and isolation. Survival depended on careful resource management and cooperation. Every member of the crew contributed to the effort.

7 Eventually, the ice began breaking apart. Shackleton ordered the crew into small lifeboats. The men navigated dangerous waters while searching for land. After a difficult journey, they reached a remote island. Although they were no longer drifting at sea, they remained far from rescue.

8 Determined to save his crew, Shackleton selected a small group of men and launched another voyage in a lifeboat. The journey required crossing hundreds of miles of rough ocean. Towering waves, storms, and freezing conditions threatened the expedition at every stage.

9 After reaching South Georgia Island, Shackleton and several companions still faced one final challenge. They crossed mountainous terrain that few people had ever explored. Only after completing this difficult trek were they able to organize a rescue mission.

10 In August 1916, nearly two years after the expedition began, Shackleton successfully returned for the remaining crew members. Remarkably, every member of the Endurance expedition survived. Although the original goal of crossing Antarctica was never achieved, the expedition became famous as one of history's greatest survival stories.

11 Today, Shackleton's leadership continues to be studied by explorers, historians, and business leaders. His ability to remain focused during adversity helped transform a failed expedition into an enduring example of perseverance and teamwork.

questions.text:
Which detail from Passage 1 provides the STRONGEST evidence that the boys contributed to their own survival before rescuers arrived?
A  "the team failed to return" (paragraph 2)
B  "they had survived by conserving energy and remaining calm" (paragraph 5)
C  "Most of the boys had little or no experience swimming in such conditions" (paragraph 6)
D  "Each boy would be accompanied by highly trained divers" (paragraph 8)

In paragraph 3 of Passage 2, the phrase "the ice tightened its grip" suggests that the ice [ ▾ ].
trapped the ship more firmly and would not release it
began to melt and free the ship slowly
moved the ship safely toward the shore
was gripped tightly by the crew's tools

Select TWO statements that accurately describe how the structures of Passage 1 and Passage 2 are similar.
A  Both passages organize events in chronological order from the start of the crisis to its resolution.
B  Both passages are arranged as a list of separate problems with no connection between them.
C  Both passages end with a concluding paragraph that reflects on the event's lasting significance.
D  Both passages are structured as a side-by-side comparison of two different rescues.
E  Both passages open by presenting an argument the author then tries to prove.

In Passage 2, how does the crushing of the Endurance (paragraph 4) most affect the events that follow?
A  It causes Shackleton to abandon his crew and seek safety alone.
B  It forces the crew to shift from sailing toward their goal to struggling to survive and escape.
C  It convinces the crew that crossing Antarctica by land is now easier than before.
D  It immediately ends the expedition by allowing a quick rescue from the ice.

writingPrompt.text: Which achievement is more impressive: the successful rescue of the soccer team in Passage 1 or Ernest Shackleton's leadership during the Endurance expedition in Passage 2? Write a multi-paragraph response in which you explain your claim, using specific evidence from both passages to support your ideas. Manage your time carefully so that you can: review the passages; plan your response; write your response; and revise and edit your response. Be sure to: introduce a clear central idea; develop your opinion with relevant reasons and details; use evidence from the passages; and organize your ideas clearly.

passages.title: The Eagle Has Landed
passages.text:
1 On July 20, 1969, millions of people around the world gathered around televisions, radios, and news reports. Some watched from crowded living rooms. Others stood in store windows or community centers where televisions had been placed for public viewing. They were all waiting for the same moment. For the first time in history, human beings were about to attempt a landing on the Moon.

2 The journey had begun several days earlier when Apollo 11 launched from Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The mission carried three astronauts: Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins. Their destination was a place no human had ever visited. Although years of planning, testing, and training had prepared them for the mission, many challenges remained.

3 As the spacecraft traveled toward the Moon, engineers and flight controllers monitored every detail. Thousands of people contributed to the mission behind the scenes. Scientists, technicians, mathematicians, and engineers had spent years solving problems that earlier generations could barely imagine.

4 Once Apollo 11 reached lunar orbit, Armstrong and Aldrin transferred into a smaller spacecraft known as the Lunar Module, nicknamed Eagle. Collins remained aboard the Command Module, circling the Moon while his crewmates prepared for the descent.

5 The landing was far from routine. As Eagle moved toward the lunar surface, warning alarms suddenly appeared. Mission Control had trained for numerous emergencies, but these alarms created uncertainty. Engineers quickly analyzed the situation and determined that the landing could continue.

6 Moments later, Armstrong realized another problem. The computer-guided landing site was filled with large rocks and boulders. If the spacecraft landed there, it could be damaged. Taking manual control, Armstrong carefully guided Eagle toward a safer location while fuel supplies continued to decrease.

7 In Houston, Mission Control watched nervously. Every second mattered. Fuel levels dropped lower and lower as Armstrong searched for a suitable landing area. Finally, a message crackled across communication systems.

8 "Houston, Tranquility Base here. The Eagle has landed."

9 Cheers erupted inside Mission Control. Around the world, people celebrated. After years of effort and uncertainty, the landing had succeeded.

10 Several hours later, Armstrong climbed down the ladder and stepped onto the Moon's surface. As cameras transmitted images back to Earth, people witnessed a moment that many considered impossible only a generation earlier.

11 Armstrong's first words became famous around the world. Soon afterward, Aldrin joined him on the lunar surface. Together, they conducted experiments, collected samples, and explored the area surrounding the landing site.

12 Although the astronauts spent only a short time on the Moon, the achievement represented decades of scientific progress and international attention. The mission demonstrated what people could accomplish through innovation, cooperation, and determination.

13 When Apollo 11 safely returned to Earth, celebrations occurred across the globe. The mission became one of the most significant events of the twentieth century. More than a successful spaceflight, it represented humanity's ability to reach beyond familiar boundaries and explore the unknown.

passages.title: Failure Is Not an Option
passages.text:
1 Less than a year after Apollo 11's success, another mission set out for the Moon. On April 11, 1970, Apollo 13 launched from Florida carrying astronauts Jim Lovell, Jack Swigert, and Fred Haise. The mission was expected to be another important step in lunar exploration. Few people realized that it would instead become one of the most dramatic survival stories in spaceflight history.

2 For the first two days, the mission appeared routine. The spacecraft traveled smoothly through space while astronauts completed scheduled tasks. Then, on April 13, everything changed.

3 An oxygen tank inside the spacecraft exploded.

4 The blast damaged critical systems and caused oxygen supplies to rapidly decrease. Almost immediately, warning lights illuminated control panels. Equipment began shutting down. The astronauts quickly understood that their mission to land on the Moon was over.

5 Back on Earth, Mission Control received a calm but alarming message from the crew.

6 "Houston, we've had a problem."

7 Those four words marked the beginning of an intense effort to save three lives. Engineers, scientists, and flight controllers immediately began searching for solutions. The damaged spacecraft could no longer operate as originally planned. Instead, the team had to develop new procedures while the astronauts were already in space.

8 To conserve power and resources, the crew moved into the Lunar Module, which was never intended to support three astronauts for such an extended period. Temperatures inside the spacecraft dropped. Water supplies became limited. Every decision required careful calculation.

9 Meanwhile, engineers on Earth worked around the clock. They tested procedures, examined technical diagrams, and searched for ways to overcome unexpected challenges. One major problem involved removing carbon dioxide from the spacecraft's air supply. The available equipment was not designed to work together.

10 Using only materials available onboard, engineers developed a solution. The astronauts followed instructions and successfully assembled an improvised device that helped keep the air breathable.

11 As Apollo 13 traveled around the Moon and began its return to Earth, uncertainty remained. The damaged spacecraft still had to survive reentry into Earth's atmosphere. A mistake during this phase could prove disastrous.

12 Finally, on April 17, mission controllers watched anxiously as the spacecraft descended toward Earth. Communication temporarily disappeared as expected during reentry. For several tense minutes, no one knew whether the crew had survived.

13 Then communication returned.

14 Soon afterward, parachutes appeared in the sky above the Pacific Ocean. The spacecraft splashed safely into the water, and recovery teams reached the astronauts.

15 Although Apollo 13 never landed on the Moon, many people consider it one of NASA's greatest achievements. The mission demonstrated the value of teamwork, problem-solving, and perseverance under extraordinary pressure.

16 What began as a failed lunar mission ended as a remarkable example of human ingenuity. Faced with a crisis hundreds of thousands of miles from Earth, astronauts and engineers worked together to overcome challenges that had never been anticipated. Their success remains one of the most inspiring chapters in the history of exploration.

questions.text:
In Text 1, which detail provides the STRONGEST evidence that the Apollo 11 landing succeeded only because of human judgment rather than automated systems alone?
A  "As the spacecraft traveled toward the Moon, engineers and flight controllers monitored every detail" (paragraph 3)
B  "Taking manual control, Armstrong carefully guided Eagle toward a safer location while fuel supplies continued to decrease" (paragraph 6)
C  "Once Apollo 11 reached lunar orbit, Armstrong and Aldrin transferred into a smaller spacecraft known as the Lunar Module" (paragraph 4)
D  "Several hours later, Armstrong climbed down the ladder and stepped onto the Moon's surface" (paragraph 10)

In Text 2, how does the incident described in paragraph 3 ("An oxygen tank inside the spacecraft exploded.") most directly propel the action of the narrative?
A  It proves that the astronauts were unprepared and poorly trained for spaceflight.
B  It shifts the mission from routine lunar exploration to an urgent effort to keep the crew alive.
C  It reveals that Mission Control had caused the malfunction through a careless error.
D  It shows that the crew immediately abandoned the spacecraft and waited for rescue.

In Text 2, paragraph 10, the astronauts assembled an "improvised device" using only materials available onboard. As used in this sentence, the word "improvised" most nearly means [ ▾ ].
created quickly from whatever was available
tested carefully over many months
purchased from a special supplier
ordered by the Moon-landing schedule

Both texts describe a famous spoken message from a spacecraft. Select two statements that accurately compare how the points of view expressed in "Houston, Tranquility Base here. The Eagle has landed." (Text 1, paragraph 8) and "Houston, we've had a problem." (Text 2, paragraph 6) differ.
A  The message in Text 1 announces a success the crew has achieved, while the message in Text 2 reports a danger the crew now faces.
B  The message in Text 1 reflects relief and accomplishment, whereas the message in Text 2 reflects calm composure during a crisis.
C  Both messages express the astronauts' anger at Mission Control for failing to prevent the problems they encountered.
D  The message in Text 1 is meant to confuse listeners, while the message in Text 2 is meant to entertain them.
E  Both messages reveal that the astronauts had given up hope of returning safely to Earth.

Be sure to: introduce a clear central idea; develop your opinion with relevant reasons and details; use evidence from the passages; and organize your ideas clearly.
